110	            KeyCode::Char('r') => Action::RulesToggle,
111	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::CursorUp,
112	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::CursorDown,
113	            KeyCode::Left | KeyCode::Char('h') => Action::CursorLeft,
114	            KeyCode::Right | KeyCode::Char('l') => Action::CursorRight,
115	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::SelectOrCommit,
116	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Cancel,
117	            KeyCode::Char('u') => Action::Undo,
118	            KeyCode::Char('f') => Action::Flip,
119	            KeyCode::Char('n') => Action::NewGame,
120	            KeyCode::Char('t') => Action::ChatStart,
121	            _ => Action::None,
122	        },
123	    }
124	}
125	
126	pub fn from_mouse(ev: MouseEvent) -> Action {
127	    match ev.kind {
128	        MouseEventKind::Down(MouseButton::Left) => {
129	            Action::Click { term_col: ev.column, term_row: ev.row }
130	        }
131	        _ => Action::None,
132	    }
133	}
134

353	    /// Compute the input mode for the current screen so main.rs can drive
354	    /// `from_key` without poking at private state.
355	    pub fn input_mode(&self) -> InputMode {
356	        match &self.screen {
357	            Screen::Picker(_) => InputMode::Picker,
358	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
359	                if l.pending_join.is_some() {
360	                    InputMode::Text
361	                } else {
362	                    InputMode::Lobby
363	                }
364	            }
365	            Screen::HostPrompt(_) | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => InputMode::Text,
366	            // While typing a chat line, hijack the keymap so printable chars
367	            // append to the buffer instead of moving the cursor.
368	            Screen::Net(n) if n.chat_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
369	            Screen::Game(_) | Screen::Net(_) => InputMode::Game,
370	        }
371	    }
372	
373	    pub fn dispatch(&mut self, action: Action) {
374	        match action {
375	            Action::None => {}
376	            Action::ConfirmYes => {
377	                self.quit_confirm_open = false;
378	                self.should_quit = true;
379	            }
380	            Action::ConfirmNo => self.quit_confirm_open = false,
381	            Action::Quit => {
382	                if self.is_game_in_progress() {
383	                    self.quit_confirm_open = true;
384	                } else {
385	                    self.should_quit = true;
386	                }
387	            }
388	            Action::HelpToggle => self.help_open = !self.help_open,
389	            Action::RulesToggle => self.rules_open = !self.rules_open,
390	            Action::NewGame => {
391	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Net(_)) {
392	                    // In Net mode, 'n' requests a rematch via the server
393	                    // instead of dropping the connection. Game must be over.
394	                    if let Screen::Net(n) = &mut self.screen {
395	                        if !n.role.map(|r| r.is_player()).unwrap_or(false) {
396	                            n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot request a rematch.".into());
397	                            return;
398	                        }
399	                        let status = n.last_view.as_ref().map(|v| v.status);
400	                        match status {
401	                            Some(GameStatus::Won { .. }) | Some(GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) => {
402	                                let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Rematch);
403	                                n.last_msg =
404	                                    Some("Rematch requested. Waiting for opponent…".into());
405	                            }
406	                            Some(GameStatus::Ongoing) => {
407	                                n.last_msg = Some(
408	                                    "'n' requests a rematch only after the game is over.".into(),
409	                                );
410	                            }
411	                            None => {
412	                                n.last_msg = Some("Not connected yet.".into());
413	                            }
414	                        }
415	                    }
416	                } else {
417	                    let style = self.style;
418	                    let use_color = self.use_color;
419	                    let observer = self.observer;
420	                    *self = AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer);
421	                }
422	            }
423	            Action::Back => self.dispatch_back(),
424	            Action::PickerUp | Action::PickerDown | Action::PickerSelect => match &self.screen {
425	                Screen::Picker(_) => self.dispatch_picker(action),
426	                Screen::Lobby(_) => self.dispatch_lobby(action),
427	                _ => {}
428	            },
429	            Action::LobbyCreate | Action::LobbyRefresh => {
430	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Lobby(_)) {
431	                    self.dispatch_lobby(action);
432	                }
433	            }
434	            Action::TextInput(_)
435	            | Action::TextBackspace
436	            | Action::FocusNext
437	            | Action::FocusPrev
438	            | Action::Submit => self.dispatch_text(action),
439	            Action::ChatStart => self.dispatch_chat_start(),
440	            Action::LobbyWatch => {
441	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Lobby(_)) {
442	                    self.dispatch_lobby_watch();
443	                }
444	            }
445	            _ => match &self.screen {
446	                Screen::Net(_) => self.dispatch_net(action),
447	                Screen::Game(_) => self.dispatch_game(action),
448	                Screen::Picker(_)
449	                | Screen::HostPrompt(_)
450	                | Screen::Lobby(_)
451	                | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => {}
452	            },
453	        }
454	    }
455

457	        let style = self.style;
458	        let use_color = self.use_color;
459	        let observer = self.observer;

456	    fn dispatch_back(&mut self) {
457	        let style = self.style;
458	        let use_color = self.use_color;
459	        let observer = self.observer;
460	        match &mut self.screen {
461	            Screen::HostPrompt(_) => {
462	                *self = AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer);
463	            }
464	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {

486	    fn dispatch_chat_start(&mut self) {
487	        let Screen::Net(n) = &mut self.screen else {
488	            return;
489	        };
490	        match n.role {
491	            Some(NetRole::Player(_)) => {
492	                n.chat_input = Some(String::new());
493	                n.last_msg = Some("Chat: type a line, Enter sends, Esc cancels.".into());
494	            }

504	    fn dispatch_lobby_watch(&mut self) {
505	        let Screen::Lobby(l) = &mut self.screen else {
506	            return;
507	        };
508	        if l.pending_join.is_some() || l.rooms.is_empty() {
509	            return;
510	        }
511	        let cursor = l.cursor.min(l.rooms.len() - 1);
512	        let room = l.rooms[cursor].clone();
513	        if room.has_password {
514	            // Spectator joins to locked rooms still need the password.
515	            l.pending_join = Some(PendingJoin {
516	                room_id: room.id,
517	                password_buf: String::new(),
518	                as_spectator: true,
519	            });
520	            l.last_msg = Some("Type password to spectate, Enter to join, Esc to cancel.".into());
521	            return;
522	        }
523	        let host = l.host.clone();
524	        let style = self.style;
525	        let use_color = self.use_color;
526	        let url = format!("{host}/ws/{}?role=spectator", room.id);
527	        *self = AppState::new_net(url, style, use_color);
528	    }

530	    fn is_game_in_progress(&self) -> bool {
531	        match &self.screen {
532	            Screen::Game(g) => {
533	                matches!(g.state.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) && !g.state.history.is_empty()
534	            }
535	            Screen::Net(n) => match &n.last_view {
536	                Some(view) => matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) && n.connected,
537	                None => false,
538	            },
539	            Screen::Picker(_)
540	            | Screen::HostPrompt(_)
541	            | Screen::Lobby(_)
542	            | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => false,
543	        }
544	    }

546	    fn dispatch_picker(&mut self, action: Action) {
547	        let Screen::Picker(p) = &mut self.screen else {
548	            return;
549	        };
550	        let n = PickerEntry::ALL.len();
551	        match action {
552	            Action::PickerUp => p.cursor = (p.cursor + n - 1) % n,
553	            Action::PickerDown => p.cursor = (p.cursor + 1) % n,
554	            Action::PickerSelect => {
555	                let entry = PickerEntry::ALL[p.cursor];
556	                let observer = self.observer;
557	                let style = self.style;
558	                let use_color = self.use_color;
559	                match entry {
560	                    PickerEntry::ConnectToServer => {
561	                        *self = AppState::new_host_prompt(style, use_color, observer);
562	                    }
563	                    PickerEntry::Quit => self.should_quit = true,
564	                    other => {
565	                        if let Some(rules) = other.rules() {
566	                            *self = AppState::new_game(rules, style, use_color, observer);
567	                        }
568	                    }
569	                }
570	            }
571	            _ => {}
572	        }
573	    }

634	    fn dispatch_text(&mut self, action: Action) {
635	        match &mut self.screen {
636	            Screen::HostPrompt(h) => match action {
637	                Action::TextInput(c) => text_input::push_char(&mut h.buf, c, 128),
638	                Action::TextBackspace => text_input::backspace(&mut h.buf),
639	                Action::Submit => {
640	                    let raw = h.buf.trim().to_string();
641	                    let host = match normalize_host_url(&raw) {
642	                        Ok(u) => u,
643	                        Err(e) => {
644	                            h.error = Some(e);
645	                            return;
646	                        }
647	                    };
648	                    let style = self.style;
649	                    let use_color = self.use_color;
650	                    let observer = self.observer;
651	                    *self = AppState::new_lobby(host, style, use_color, observer);
652	                }
653	                _ => {}
654	            },
655	            Screen::CreateRoom(c) => match action {
656	                Action::TextInput(ch) => match c.focus {
657	                    CreateRoomField::Id => text_input::push_char(&mut c.id_buf, ch, 32),
658	                    CreateRoomField::Password => text_input::push_char(&mut c.password_buf, ch, 64),
659	                    CreateRoomField::Submit => {
660	                        if matches!(ch, ' ' | '\n') {
661	                            self.dispatch_text(Action::Submit);
662	                        }
663	                    }
664	                },
665	                Action::TextBackspace => match c.focus {
666	                    CreateRoomField::Id => text_input::backspace(&mut c.id_buf),
667	                    CreateRoomField::Password => text_input::backspace(&mut c.password_buf),
668	                    _ => {}
669	                },
670	                Action::FocusNext => {
671	                    c.focus = match c.focus {
672	                        CreateRoomField::Id => CreateRoomField::Password,
673	                        CreateRoomField::Password => CreateRoomField::Submit,
674	                        CreateRoomField::Submit => CreateRoomField::Id,
675	                    };
676	                }
677	                Action::FocusPrev => {
678	                    c.focus = match c.focus {
679	                        CreateRoomField::Id => CreateRoomField::Submit,
680	                        CreateRoomField::Password => CreateRoomField::Id,
681	                        CreateRoomField::Submit => CreateRoomField::Password,
682	                    };
683	                }
684	                Action::Submit => {
685	                    let id = c.id_buf.trim().to_string();
686	                    if !valid_room_id(&id) {
687	                        c.error = Some("Room id must be 1–32 chars of [a-zA-Z0-9_-].".into());
688	                        return;
689	                    }
690	                    let host = c.host.clone();
691	                    let password =
692	                        if c.password_buf.is_empty() { None } else { Some(c.password_buf.clone()) };
693	                    let url = match password {
694	                        Some(pw) => format!("{host}/ws/{id}?password={}", urlencode(&pw)),
695	                        None => format!("{host}/ws/{id}"),
696	                    };
697	                    let style = self.style;
698	                    let use_color = self.use_color;
699	                    *self = AppState::new_net(url, style, use_color);
700	                }
701	                _ => {}
702	            },
703	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
704	                let Some(pj) = l.pending_join.as_mut() else {
705	                    return;
706	                };
707	                match action {
708	                    Action::TextInput(c) => text_input::push_char(&mut pj.password_buf, c, 64),
709	                    Action::TextBackspace => text_input::backspace(&mut pj.password_buf),
710	                    Action::Submit => {
711	                        let host = l.host.clone();
712	                        let pj_owned = l.pending_join.take().unwrap();
713	                        let mut url = format!(
714	                            "{host}/ws/{}?password={}",
715	                            pj_owned.room_id,
716	                            urlencode(&pj_owned.password_buf)
717	                        );
718	                        if pj_owned.as_spectator {
719	                            url.push_str("&role=spectator");
720	                        }
721	                        let style = self.style;
722	                        let use_color = self.use_color;
723	                        *self = AppState::new_net(url, style, use_color);
724	                    }
725	                    _ => {}
726	                }
727	            }
728	            // Net mode hijacks Text input while the user is composing a chat
729	            // line. Submit fires Chat over the wire; Esc / Back cancels (see
730	            // dispatch_back).
731	            Screen::Net(n) => {
732	                let Some(buf) = n.chat_input.as_mut() else {
733	                    return;
734	                };
735	                match action {
736	                    Action::TextInput(c) => text_input::push_char(buf, c, CHAT_INPUT_MAX),
737	                    Action::TextBackspace => text_input::backspace(buf),
738	                    Action::Submit => {
739	                        let buf = n.chat_input.take().unwrap_or_default();
740	                        let trimmed = buf.trim();
741	                        if trimmed.is_empty() {
742	                            n.last_msg = Some("Empty chat — nothing sent.".into());
743	                            return;
744	                        }
745	                        let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Chat { text: trimmed.to_string() });
746	                        n.last_msg = Some("Chat sent.".into());
747	                    }
748	                    _ => {}
749	                }
750	            }
751	            _ => {}
752	        }
753	    }
754	
755	    fn dispatch_game(&mut self, action: Action) {
756	        let Screen::Game(g) = &mut self.screen else {
757	            return;
758	        };
759	        let shape = g.state.board.shape();
760	        let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
761	        match action {
762	            Action::CursorUp if g.cursor.0 > 0 => {
763	                g.cursor.0 -= 1;
764	            }
765	            Action::CursorDown if g.cursor.0 + 1 < rows => {
766	                g.cursor.0 += 1;
767	            }
768	            Action::CursorLeft if g.cursor.1 > 0 => {
769	                g.cursor.1 -= 1;
770	            }
771	            Action::CursorRight if g.cursor.1 + 1 < cols => {
772	                g.cursor.1 += 1;
773	            }
774	            Action::Cancel => {
775	                g.selected = None;
776	                g.last_msg = None;
777	            }
778	            Action::SelectOrCommit => {
779	                let observer = self.observer;
780	                Self::handle_select_or_commit(g, observer);
781	            }
782	            Action::Undo => match g.state.unmake_move() {
783	                Ok(()) => {
784	                    g.state.refresh_status();
785	                    g.selected = None;
786	                    g.last_msg = Some(format!("Undone. {:?} to move.", g.state.side_to_move));
787	                }
788	                Err(e) => {
789	                    g.last_msg = Some(format!("Cannot undo: {e}"));
```

821	    fn handle_select_or_commit(g: &mut GameView, observer: Side) {
822	        if !matches!(g.state.status, chess_core::state::GameStatus::Ongoing) {
823	            g.last_msg =
824	                Some("Game over. Press 'n' for new game, 'u' to take back, 'q' to quit.".into());
825	            return;
826	        }
827	        let shape = g.state.board.shape();
828	        let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(g.cursor.0, g.cursor.1, observer, shape) else {
829	            g.last_msg = Some("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
830	            return;
831	        };
832	
833	        let view = PlayerView::project(&g.state, g.state.side_to_move);
834	
835	        match g.selected {
836	            None => {
837	                // Try to select a piece. Allowed if there's a legal move from this square.
838	                let any = view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq);
839	                if any {
840	                    g.selected = Some(sq);
841	                    g.last_msg = None;
842	                } else {
843	                    // Maybe it's a banqi hidden cell — suggest 'f' to flip.
844	                    match g.state.board.get(sq) {
845	                        Some(p) if !p.revealed => {
846	                            g.last_msg = Some("Hidden piece. Press 'f' to flip.".into());
847	                        }
848	                        _ => g.last_msg = Some("No legal move from that square.".into()),
849	                    }
850	                }
851	            }
852	            Some(from) if from == sq => {
853	                g.selected = None;
854	                g.last_msg = None;
855	            }
856	            Some(from) => {
857	                // Find a legal move from `from` to `sq`. Prefer Capture / CannonJump
858	                // over a chain ending at sq (for banqi); take the first match.
859	                let candidate = view
860	                    .legal_moves
861	                    .iter()
862	                    .find(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(sq))
863	                    .cloned();
864	                match candidate {
865	                    Some(m) => Self::apply_move(g, m),
866	                    None => {
867	                        g.last_msg = Some("Illegal move.".into());
868	                        g.selected = None;
869	                    }
870	                }
871	            }
872	        }
873	    }
874	
875	    fn apply_move(g: &mut GameView, m: Move) {
876	        match g.state.make_move(&m) {
877	            Ok(()) => {
878	                g.state.refresh_status();
879	                g.selected = None;
880	                g.last_msg = Some(format!(
881	                    "Played: {}",
882	                    chess_core::notation::iccs::encode_move(&g.state.board, &m)
883	                ));
884	            }
885	            Err(e) => {
886	                g.last_msg = Some(format!("Engine rejected move: {e}"));
887	                g.selected = None;
888	            }
889	        }
890	    }
891	
892	    fn dispatch_net(&mut self, action: Action) {
893	        let Screen::Net(n) = &mut self.screen else {
894	            return;
895	        };
896	        // Pre-welcome: no view yet, nothing to dispatch on.
897	        let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else {
898	            return;
899	        };
900	        let shape = view.shape;
901	        let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
902	        let role = n.role.unwrap_or(NetRole::Player(Side::RED));
903	        let observer = role.observer();
904	        match action {
905	            Action::CursorUp if n.cursor.0 > 0 => {
906	                n.cursor.0 -= 1;
907	            }
908	            Action::CursorDown if n.cursor.0 + 1 < rows => {
909	                n.cursor.0 += 1;
910	            }
911	            Action::CursorLeft if n.cursor.1 > 0 => {
912	                n.cursor.1 -= 1;
913	            }
914	            Action::CursorRight if n.cursor.1 + 1 < cols => {
915	                n.cursor.1 += 1;
916	            }
917	            Action::Cancel => {
918	                n.selected = None;
919	                n.last_msg = None;
920	            }
921	            Action::SelectOrCommit => {
922	                if role.is_spectator() {
923	                    n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
924	                    return;
925	                }
926	                let outcome = compute_select_outcome(n, observer);
927	                apply_select_outcome(n, outcome);
928	            }
929	            Action::Flip => {
930	                if role.is_spectator() {
931	                    n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
932	                    return;
933	                }
934	                if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
935	                    n.last_msg = Some("Game over.".into());
936	                    return;
937	                }
938	                if view.side_to_move != observer {
939	                    n.last_msg = Some("Not your turn.".into());
940	                    return;
941	                }
942	                let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(n.cursor.0, n.cursor.1, observer, shape)
943	                else {
944	                    n.last_msg = Some("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
945	                    return;
946	                };
947	                let _ = n
948	                    .client
949	                    .cmd_tx
950	                    .send(ClientMsg::Move { mv: Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None } });
951	                n.last_msg = Some("Reveal sent.".into());
952	            }
953	            Action::Undo => {
954	                n.last_msg = Some("Undo not supported in online mode yet.".into());
955	            }
956	            Action::Click { term_col, term_row } => {
957	                if role.is_spectator() {
958	                    return;
959	                }
960	                if let Some(rect) = self.board_rect {
961	                    if let Some((row, col)) = hit_test(rect, term_col, term_row, rows, cols) {
962	                        let n = match &mut self.screen {
963	                            Screen::Net(b) => b,
964	                            _ => return,
965	                        };
966	                        n.cursor = (row, col);
967	                        let outcome = compute_select_outcome(n, observer);
968	                        apply_select_outcome(n, outcome);
969	                    }
970	                }
971	            }
972	            _ => {}
973	        }
974	    }
975	}
976	
977	enum SelectOutcome {
978	    Ignore,
979	    Msg(String),
980	    ClearAndMsg(String),
981	    Select(Square),
982	    Deselect,
983	    Commit(Move),
984	}
985	
986	fn compute_select_outcome(n: &NetView, observer: Side) -> SelectOutcome {
987	    let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else {
988	        return SelectOutcome::Ignore;
989	    };
990	    if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
991	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Game over.".into());
992	    }
993	    let shape = view.shape;
994	    let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(n.cursor.0, n.cursor.1, observer, shape) else {
995	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
996	    };
997	    if view.side_to_move != observer {
998	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Not your turn.".into());
999	    }
1000	    match n.selected {
1001	        None => {
1002	            if view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq) {
1003	                SelectOutcome::Select(sq)
1004	            } else if matches!(view.cells[sq.0 as usize], VisibleCell::Hidden) {
1005	                SelectOutcome::Msg("Hidden piece. Press 'f' to flip.".into())
1006	            } else {
1007	                SelectOutcome::Msg("No legal move from that square.".into())
1008	            }
1009	        }

1025	fn apply_select_outcome(n: &mut NetView, outcome: SelectOutcome) {
1026	    match outcome {
1027	        SelectOutcome::Ignore => {}
1028	        SelectOutcome::Msg(m) => n.last_msg = Some(m),
1029	        SelectOutcome::ClearAndMsg(m) => {
1030	            n.selected = None;
1031	            n.last_msg = Some(m);
1032	        }
1033	        SelectOutcome::Select(sq) => {
1034	            n.selected = Some(sq);
1035	            n.last_msg = None;
1036	        }
1037	        SelectOutcome::Deselect => {
1038	            n.selected = None;
1039	            n.last_msg = None;
1040	        }
1041	        SelectOutcome::Commit(mv) => {
1042	            let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
1043	            n.selected = None;
1044	            n.last_msg = Some("Sent.".into());
1045	        }
1046	    }
1047	}
1048	
1049	fn apply_net_event(n: &mut NetView, evt: NetEvent) {
1050	    match evt {
1051	        NetEvent::Connected => {
1052	            n.connected = true;
1053	            n.last_msg = Some("Connected. Waiting for welcome…".into());
1054	        }
1055	        NetEvent::Server(boxed) => match *boxed {
1056	            ServerMsg::Hello { observer, rules, view, .. } => {
1057	                let was_seated = n.role.is_some();
1058	                n.role = Some(NetRole::Player(observer));
1059	                n.rules = Some(rules);
1060	                let shape = view.shape;
1061	                let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
1062	                n.cursor = (rows / 2, cols / 2);
1063	                n.selected = None;
1064	                n.last_view = Some(view);
1065	                n.connected = true;
1066	                // First Hello = "Joined as X". Subsequent Hello = rematch reset.
1067	                n.last_msg = Some(if was_seated {
1068	                    "Rematch — new game.".into()
1069	                } else {
1070	                    format!("Joined as {}.", side_label(observer))
1071	                });
1072	            }
1073	            ServerMsg::Spectating { rules, view, .. } => {

1075	                n.role = Some(NetRole::Spectator);
1076	                n.rules = Some(rules);
1077	                let shape = view.shape;

1079	                n.cursor = (rows / 2, cols / 2);
1080	                n.selected = None;

1085	                } else {

```text
1	//! ICCS notation: file `a..i` × rank `0..9` → `h2e2` style.
2	//!
3	//! Encoder is straightforward; decoder takes a state because string-form
4	//! moves are ambiguous between Step/Capture/CannonJump (the geometry is
5	//! identical from outside) — we resolve by intersecting with `legal_moves`.
6	
7	use crate::board::Board;
8	use crate::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
9	use crate::error::CoreError;
10	use crate::moves::Move;
11	use crate::state::GameState;
12	
13	/// Encode a single square as `<file><rank>` e.g. `h2`.
14	pub fn encode_square(board: &Board, sq: Square) -> String {
15	    let (f, r) = board.file_rank(sq);
16	    format!("{}{}", file_char(f.0), r.0)
17	}
18	
19	fn file_char(f: u8) -> char {
20	    (b'a' + f) as char
21	}
22	
23	fn parse_square(board: &Board, s: &str) -> Result<Square, CoreError> {
24	    let mut chars = s.chars();
25	    let f = chars.next().ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))?;
26	    let r = chars.next().ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))?;
27	    if chars.next().is_some() {
28	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()));
29	    }
30	    if !f.is_ascii_lowercase() {
31	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()));
32	    }
33	    let file_idx = (f as u8) - b'a';
34	    let rank_idx = r.to_digit(10).ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))? as u8;
35	    if file_idx >= board.width() || rank_idx >= board.height() {
36	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(format!("{s} out of board bounds")));
37	    }
38	    Ok(board.sq(File(file_idx), Rank(rank_idx)))
39	}
40	
41	/// Encode a move as ICCS. `Reveal` becomes `flip <sq>`. `ChainCapture`
42	/// chains squares with `x` separators.
43	pub fn encode_move(board: &Board, m: &Move) -> String {
44	    match m {
45	        Move::Reveal { at, .. } => format!("flip {}", encode_square(board, *at)),
46	        Move::Step { from, to } => {
47	            format!("{}{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
48	        }
49	        Move::Capture { from, to, .. } | Move::CannonJump { from, to, .. } => {
50	            format!("{}x{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
51	        }
52	        Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
53	            let mut s = encode_square(board, *from);
54	            for hop in path {
55	                s.push('x');
56	                s.push_str(&encode_square(board, hop.to));
57	            }
58	            s
59	        }
60	    }
61	}
62	
63	/// Decode a move from ICCS, resolving Step/Capture ambiguity by matching
64	/// against the current legal-move list. Forms accepted:
65	/// - `h2e2`               — step
66	/// - `h2xh9`              — capture (single)
67	/// - `a3xb3xc3`           — chain capture (any number of x-separated squares)
68	/// - `flip a3` / `flip a3` — reveal
69	pub fn decode_move(state: &GameState, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
70	    let trimmed = input.trim();
71	    if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("flip ") {
72	        let at = parse_square(&state.board, rest.trim())?;
73	        return Ok(Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None });
74	    }
75	    let board = &state.board;
76	
77	    // Split into squares: handle both 'h2e2' (no separator) and 'h2xe2'.
78	    let parts: Vec<Square> = if trimmed.contains('x') {
79	        trimmed.split('x').map(|p| parse_square(board, p.trim())).collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?
80	    } else if trimmed.len() == 4 {
81	        vec![parse_square(board, &trimmed[..2])?, parse_square(board, &trimmed[2..])?]
82	    } else {
83	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(input.into()));
84	    };
85	
86	    if parts.len() < 2 {
87	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(input.into()));
88	    }
89	
90	    let from = parts[0];
91	    let legal = state.legal_moves();
92	
93	    if parts.len() == 2 {
94	        // Step / Capture / CannonJump — find unique match by (from, to).
95	        let to = parts[1];
96	        let candidates: Vec<&Move> = legal
97	            .iter()
98	            .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(to))
99	            .collect();
100	        match candidates.as_slice() {
101	            [one] => Ok((*one).clone()),
102	            [] => Err(CoreError::Illegal("no legal move matches notation")),
103	            _multi => {
104	                // Multiple matches (e.g. Step vs ChainCapture with len 2). Prefer
105	                // the simplest: Step > Capture > CannonJump > ChainCapture.
106	                let pick = candidates
107	                    .iter()
108	                    .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. }))
109	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Capture { .. })))
110	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::CannonJump { .. })))
111	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { .. })));
112	                pick.map(|m| (*m).clone()).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("ambiguous"))
113	            }
114	        }
115	    } else {
116	        // ChainCapture — find legal move whose path matches.
117	        let chain_squares = &parts[1..];
118	        let candidate = legal.iter().find(|m| match m {
119	            Move::ChainCapture { from: f, path } => {
120	                *f == from
121	                    && path.len() == chain_squares.len()
122	                    && path.iter().zip(chain_squares.iter()).all(|(h, sq)| h.to == *sq)
123	            }
124	            _ => false,
125	        });
126	        candidate.cloned().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no chain capture matches notation"))
127	    }
128	}
129	
130	#[cfg(test)]
131	mod tests {
132	    use super::*;
133	    use crate::rules::RuleSet;
134	
135	    #[test]
136	    fn round_trip_simple_step() {
137	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
138	        // Pick a legal Step and round-trip it.
139	        let m = state.legal_moves().into_iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. })).unwrap();
140	        let s = encode_move(&state.board, &m);
141	        let decoded = decode_move(&state, &s).unwrap();
142	        assert_eq!(m, decoded);
143	    }
144	
145	    #[test]
146	    fn parse_h2e2_in_xiangqi() {
147	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
148	        // h2 is a Red cannon. h2e2 moves it 6 squares west — the standard 炮二平五 (well, 炮 to e2).
149	        let m = decode_move(&state, "h2e2").unwrap();
150	        if let Move::Step { from, to } = m {

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 07:07:42Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Which key should trigger coord-input mode in the TUI?**

Options:
- **: (colon, vim-style)** - Familiar to vim users; doesn't collide with hjkl/u/f/n/r/?/q/t/Enter/Esc. Prompt would render as ':h2e2_' along the bottom of the sidebar.
- **m (for 'move')** - Mnemonic. Currently unbound in Game/Net mode. Less crowded with vim conventions but uses up a single-letter slot.
- **/ (slash)** - Search-style prompt feel. Currently unbound. Some users may instinctively expect / to be search/find-piece in the future.

**Where should coord-input be available?**

Options:
- **Local + Net (both)** - Works in single-player Game screen and online Net screen. Spectators get a 'spectators cannot move' message just like the existing flip/move flow.
- **Local Game only** - Skip Net for now. Simpler to ship — no need to coordinate with the 't' chat-input hijack in NetView. Net would be a P1 follow-up.

**How should the input prompt render?**

Options:
- **Bottom of the sidebar (yellow line)** - Mirrors how chat-input renders today. Reuses an existing visual idiom; users already trained on it. Adds one row to the sidebar when active.
- **Floating overlay above the board** - Centered modal-style prompt; more visually prominent. Costs more layout work and a new render path.


**Answer:** Maybe support : for instant prompt + move / m for parse 坐標 in real time (so user can see what they selected and where they will go, and enter to confirm, Local + Net (both), Bottom of the sidebar (yellow line)" selected preview:
│ Status                  │
│ Variant: Xiangqi (象棋)  │
│ Side to move: Red       │
│ Selected: —             │
│                         │
│ ?=help, r=rules, n=new  │
│ > h2e2_                 │
└─────────────────────────┘
</details></tool-use>

Design coord-input plan

```text
Excellent. I have all the facts I need. Now I'll produce the implementation plan.

---

# TUI Coordinate-Based Move Input — Implementation Plan

## Goal recap

Add two trigger keys in `InputMode::Game` for typing ICCS moves directly:

- **`:`** — *Instant prompt* mode. Text-only buffer; no live preview; Enter commits; Esc cancels.
- **`m`** — *Live preview* mode. Each keystroke re-parses; a 2-char prefix sets `selected = Some(square)` so the existing selected-square highlight kicks in; a full 4-char move also moves the cursor onto the destination square so the cursor highlight previews the target. Esc restores the cursor + selection that existed before `m` was pressed.

Both work in **Local Game** (`Screen::Game`) and **Net** (`Screen::Net`); spectators are gated with the existing `"Spectators cannot move."` message and the prompt never opens.

---

## 1. State additions

A single shared struct, parameterized by mode, lives directly on each view (mirrors `chat_input: Option<String>`).

**New helper enum + struct in `clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs`:**

```rust
#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
pub enum CoordKind { Instant, Live }

pub struct CoordInputState {
    pub kind: CoordKind,
    pub buf: String,
    /// Live mode only: (cursor, selected) at entry, restored on Esc.
    /// Always `None` when `kind == Instant`.
    pub snapshot: Option<((u8, u8), Option<Square>)>,
}
```

Place it next to `PendingJoin` and `CreateRoomField`; make the struct fields `pub` so dispatchers and the renderer can read them.

**`GameView`** (line 80-85) gains:
```rust
pub coord_input: Option<CoordInputState>,
```

**`NetView`** (line 115-133) gains the same field after `chat_input`:
```rust
pub coord_input: Option<CoordInputState>,
```

Initialize to `None` in `AppState::new_game` and `AppState::new_net`. Reset to `None` whenever either view is rebuilt.

**Why on the view, not separate**: the mutual-exclusion gate against `chat_input` (Net only) is cleanest when both fields sit on the same struct; the `input_mode()` and `dispatch_back()` arms can do `n.chat_input.is_some() || n.coord_input.is_some()` in one match.

**Buffer cap**: `const COORD_INPUT_MAX: usize = 16;` near `CHAT_INPUT_MAX` (longest realistic ICCS string is `flip a0` = 7 chars; chain-capture `a3xb3xc3xd3` = 11; 16 leaves headroom).

---

## 2. Action additions

**Single new variant** in `clients/chess-tui/src/input.rs`:

```rust
/// Open the coordinate-input prompt (Game / Net, players only).
CoordStart(CoordKind),
```

Plus `pub use` of `CoordKind` from `app.rs` — actually cleaner to **define `CoordKind` inside `input.rs`** and re-export it from `app.rs` (avoids a circular dep since `app.rs` already imports from `input.rs`). Move:

```rust
// in input.rs
#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
pub enum CoordKind { Instant, Live }
```

Then `app.rs` imports it via `use crate::input::{Action, CoordKind, InputMode};` and defines `CoordInputState` locally.

**Keymap delta** in `from_key` `InputMode::Game` arm (around line 120, alongside `'t' => ChatStart`):

```rust
KeyCode::Char(':') => Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Instant),
KeyCode::Char('m') => Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Live),
```

No InputMode::Text changes — the Text branch already handles `TextInput / TextBackspace / Submit / Back`.

---

## 3. `input_mode()` extension

In `app.rs:355-371`, extend the `Net(n)` branch and add a `Game(g)` branch (currently only `chat_input` is checked):

```rust
Screen::Net(n) if n.chat_input.is_some() || n.coord_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
Screen::Game(g) if g.coord_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
Screen::Game(_) | Screen::Net(_) => InputMode::Game,
```

---

## 4. Action routing in `dispatch()`

Add a new arm in `AppState::dispatch` (line 373-454), parallel to `ChatStart`:

```rust
Action::CoordStart(kind) => self.dispatch_coord_start(kind),
```

---

## 5. New `dispatch_coord_start`

Mirrors `dispatch_chat_start` (line 486-502) but spans both screens.

```rust
fn dispatch_coord_start(&mut self, kind: CoordKind) {
    match &mut self.screen {
        Screen::Game(g) => {
            // Local: open unconditionally; engine gates illegal moves on submit.
            // (mirror of handle_select_or_commit's GameStatus::Ongoing check is
            // applied at submit time, not entry time — keeps the prompt useful
            // for, e.g., post-game inspection of typing.)
            g.coord_input = Some(CoordInputState {
                kind,
                buf: String::new(),
                snapshot: match kind {
                    CoordKind::Live => Some((g.cursor, g.selected)),
                    CoordKind::Instant => None,
                },
            });
            // Live mode clears the highlight to start clean; Instant leaves it.
            if matches!(kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                g.selected = None;
            }
            g.last_msg = Some(coord_help_msg(kind));
        }
        Screen::Net(n) => {
            // Mutual exclusion with chat-input.
            if n.chat_input.is_some() {
                n.last_msg = Some("Finish or cancel chat (Esc) before move-input.".into());
                return;
            }
            // Spectator gate (mirrors line 495-497).
            match n.role {
                Some(NetRole::Spectator) => {
                    n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
                    return;
                }
                None => {
                    n.last_msg = Some("Not connected yet.".into());
                    return;
                }
                Some(NetRole::Player(_)) => {}
            }
            n.coord_input = Some(CoordInputState {
                kind,
                buf: String::new(),
                snapshot: match kind {
                    CoordKind::Live => Some((n.cursor, n.selected)),
                    CoordKind::Instant => None,
                },
            });
            if matches!(kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                n.selected = None;
            }
            n.last_msg = Some(coord_help_msg(kind));
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}

fn coord_help_msg(kind: CoordKind) -> String {
    match kind {
        CoordKind::Instant => "Coord (instant): type ICCS, Enter commits, Esc cancels.".into(),
        CoordKind::Live    => "Coord (live): type ICCS, Enter commits, Esc cancels.".into(),
    }
}
```

Mutual-exclusion in the other direction: extend `dispatch_chat_start` with `if n.coord_input.is_some() { n.last_msg = Some("Finish or cancel coord-input (Esc) before chat.".into()); return; }` after the `Screen::Net(n)` early-let.

## 6. `dispatch_text` extension

Extend the `Screen::Net(n)` branch (line 731-750) and add a `Screen::Game(g)` branch.

For **Net**, replace the `let Some(buf) = n.chat_input.as_mut() else { return; };` with a dispatch on which input mode is active:

```rust
Screen::Net(n) => {
    if n.coord_input.is_some() {
        Self::dispatch_coord_text_net(n, action);
    } else if n.chat_input.is_some() {
        // existing chat branch verbatim
    }
}
```

For **Game**, add:

```rust
Screen::Game(g) => {
    if g.coord_input.is_some() {
        let observer = self.observer;
        Self::dispatch_coord_text_game(g, action, observer);
    }
}
```

### 6a. Local-game variant `dispatch_coord_text_game`

```rust
fn dispatch_coord_text_game(g: &mut GameView, action: Action, observer: Side) {
    let Some(ci) = g.coord_input.as_mut() else { return; };
    match action {
        Action::TextInput(c) => {
            text_input::push_char(&mut ci.buf, c, COORD_INPUT_MAX);
            if matches!(ci.kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                Self::live_preview_game(g, observer);
            }
        }
        Action::TextBackspace => {
            text_input::backspace(&mut ci.buf);
            if matches!(ci.kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                Self::live_preview_game(g, observer);
            }
        }
        Action::Submit => {
            // Take ownership of buf, leave struct in place for error case.
            let buf = std::mem::take(&mut ci.buf);
            match chess_core::notation::iccs::decode_move(&g.state, buf.trim()) {
                Ok(mv) => {
                    g.coord_input = None;
                    Self::apply_move(g, mv);
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    // Error: keep the prompt open so the user can fix; restore buf.
                    if let Some(ci) = g.coord_input.as_mut() {
                        ci.buf = buf;
                    }
                    g.last_msg = Some(format!("Bad move: {e}"));
                }
            }
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

`live_preview_game` re-runs the buffer through a small parse helper (see §10) and updates `g.selected` and (on a 4-char success) `g.cursor` via `orient::project_cell(sq, observer, shape)`.

### 6b. Net variant `dispatch_coord_text_net`

```rust
fn dispatch_coord_text_net(n: &mut NetView, action: Action) {
    let Some(ci) = n.coord_input.as_mut() else { return; };
    match action {
        Action::TextInput(c) => {
            text_input::push_char(&mut ci.buf, c, COORD_INPUT_MAX);
            if matches!(ci.kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                live_preview_net(n);
            }
        }
        Action::TextBackspace => {
            text_input::backspace(&mut ci.buf);
            if matches!(ci.kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                live_preview_net(n);
            }
        }
        Action::Submit => {
            // Pre-flight gates: turn / status / spectator (last redundant — gate
            // on entry — but harmless and protects against role changes mid-edit).
            let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else {
                n.last_msg = Some("Not connected yet.".into());
                return;
            };
            let role = n.role.unwrap_or(NetRole::Player(Side::RED));
            if role.is_spectator() {
                n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
                n.coord_input = None;
                return;
            }
            if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
                n.last_msg = Some("Game over.".into());
                n.coord_input = None;
                return;
            }
            if view.side_to_move != role.observer() {
                n.last_msg = Some("Not your turn.".into());
                return; // keep buf so user can wait + resubmit
            }
            let buf = std::mem::take(&mut ci.buf);
            match chess_core::notation::iccs::decode_move_from_view(view, buf.trim()) {
                Ok(mv) => {
                    n.coord_input = None;
                    n.selected = None;
                    let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
                    n.last_msg = Some("Sent.".into());
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    if let Some(ci) = n.coord_input.as_mut() {
                        ci.buf = buf;
                    }
                    n.last_msg = Some(format!("Bad move: {e}"));
                }
            }
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

### 6c. Live preview helpers

```rust
fn live_preview_game(g: &mut GameView, observer: Side) {
    let Some(ci) = g.coord_input.as_ref() else { return; };
    let shape = g.state.board.shape();
    let board = &g.state.board;
    apply_live_preview(
        &ci.buf,
        |s| parse_square_from_board(board, s),
        shape,
        observer,
        &mut g.cursor,
        &mut g.selected,
    );
}

fn live_preview_net(n: &mut NetView) {
    let Some(ci) = n.coord_input.as_ref() else { return; };
    let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else { return; };
    let shape = view.shape;
    let observer = n.role.map(|r| r.observer()).unwrap_or(Side::RED);
    apply_live_preview(
        &ci.buf,
        |s| parse_square_from_view(view, s),
        shape,
        observer,
        &mut n.cursor,
        &mut n.selected,
    );
}

/// Re-parse `buf` and update (selected, cursor) for live preview.
/// - 0–1 chars or first char invalid    → selected = None, cursor unchanged
/// - 2–3 chars with valid square prefix → selected = Some(origin), cursor unchanged
/// - 4 chars with valid from+to         → selected = Some(origin), cursor = display_at(to)
/// - "flip <sq>" or chain (5+)          → selected stays at last valid origin
fn apply_live_preview<F>(
    buf: &str,
    mut parse_sq: F,
    shape: BoardShape,
    observer: Side,
    cursor: &mut (u8, u8),
    selected: &mut Option<Square>,
) where F: FnMut(&str) -> Option<Square> {
    let trimmed = buf.trim_start_matches("flip ");
    let bare = trimmed.split('x').next().unwrap_or("");
    let len = trimmed.chars().count();
    // Drop highlight on a clean / too-short buffer.
    if buf.is_empty() || (len < 2 && !buf.starts_with("flip")) {
        *selected = None;
        return;
    }
    if let Some(origin) = parse_sq(&trimmed[..trimmed.len().min(2)]) {
        *selected = Some(origin);
        // Full 4-char form (and not "flip "): also jump the cursor.
        if buf.len() == 4 && !buf.contains('x') {
            if let Some(dest) = parse_sq(&trimmed[2..4]) {
                let (r, c) = orient::project_cell(dest, observer, shape);
                *cursor = (r, c);
            }
        }
        // After x-separator (e.g. "h2xh9"): also jump the cursor on the first hop.
        if buf.contains('x') {
            let parts: Vec<&str> = buf.split('x').collect();
            if parts.len() == 2 && parts[1].len() == 2 {
                if let Some(dest) = parse_sq(parts[1]) {
                    let (r, c) = orient::project_cell(dest, observer, shape);
                    *cursor = (r, c);
                }
            }
        }
        let _ = bare;
    } else {
        *selected = None;
    }
}
```

(The function deliberately stays syntactically tolerant: parse failures simply don't advance the highlight; the user can backspace and retry without an error popup.)

## 7. `dispatch_back` extension

Add coord-cancel arms before the chat arm at line 478:

```rust
Screen::Game(g) if g.coord_input.is_some() => {
    let ci = g.coord_input.take().unwrap();
    if let Some((cur, sel)) = ci.snapshot {
        g.cursor = cur;
        g.selected = sel;
    }
    g.last_msg = None;
}
Screen::Net(n) if n.coord_input.is_some() => {
    let ci = n.coord_input.take().unwrap();
    if let Some((cur, sel)) = ci.snapshot {
        n.cursor = cur;
        n.selected = sel;
    }
    n.last_msg = None;
}
// existing chat arm:
Screen::Net(n) if n.chat_input.is_some() => { … }
```

Order matters: coord-cancel arms come **before** the chat-cancel arm so the active coord prompt wins over a (impossible by mutual exclusion, but defensively) lingering chat field.

For Instant mode `snapshot` is `None` so no restore; cursor + selected stay as they were since live-preview never touched them.

## 8. Where the parsing logic lives

**Recommend (a)**: add `chess_core::notation::iccs::decode_move_from_view` and refactor `decode_move` to share. Reasoning:

- The TUI already pulls `chess-core::notation::iccs` for `encode_move` and `encode_square`; adding a sibling decode is a tiny surface change.
- Keeps the Step/Capture/CannonJump precedence logic in one place; the `chain_capture` resolver is the most subtle bit and should not be duplicated.
- `chess-web` will eventually want the same — putting it in chess-core makes it reusable.

### chess-core change

Refactor `iccs.rs` so the legal-move-resolution part takes a slice and a board, not a `&GameState`:

```rust
// Existing public entry stays, but delegates.
pub fn decode_move(state: &GameState, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
    decode_move_inner(&state.board, &state.legal_moves(), input)
}

pub fn decode_move_from_view(view: &PlayerView, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
    // Build a temporary Board-like from view.shape so parse_square works.
    // PlayerView already has width/height/shape. parse_square only consults
    // board.width()/height() and board.sq(File, Rank). We can either:
    //   (i) add `parse_square_dims(width, height, shape, s)` and use it, or
    //   (ii) reconstruct a Board::empty(view.shape) for parsing only.
    // (i) is cheaper. Suggested signature:
    decode_move_dims(view.shape, view.width, view.height, &view.legal_moves, input)
}

fn decode_move_inner(board: &Board, legal: &[Move], input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> { ... }
fn decode_move_dims(shape: BoardShape, w: u8, h: u8, legal: &[Move], input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> { ... }
```

Alternative: introduce a private `parse_square_dims(width: u8, height: u8, shape: BoardShape, s: &str) -> Result<Square, CoreError>` (the existing `parse_square` only differs in that it reads `board.width()/height()` and constructs `Square` via `board.sq(File, Rank)` — the latter is just `Square((rank * width + file) as u16)` for non-Custom shapes; check `board::sq` to confirm it has shape-dependent masking and, if so, also expose `BoardShape::sq(file, rank) -> Square` or pass `shape` and reuse).

## 11. CLAUDE.md updates

### 11a. Input-map paragraph (line 84-91)

Append a sentence:
> `:` opens an instant coord-input prompt (type ICCS like `h2e2` / `a3xb3xc3` / `flip a0`, Enter commits, Esc cancels); `m` opens the same with live preview — every keystroke updates the selected-square highlight, and a complete 4-char move also previews the destination via the cursor.

### 11b. Gotchas section (after line 139, the chat-input gotcha)

Add:
> - **chess-tui coord-input mode also hijacks the keymap (Game + Net).** `:` and `m` set `coord_input = Some(CoordInputState { … })`; `app.input_mode()` returns `InputMode::Text`. `m` snapshots `(cursor, selected)` so Esc can restore the highlight; `:` does not. **Coord-input and chat-input are mutually exclusive in Net** — opening one with the other active is rejected with a `last_msg`. Decoding goes through `chess_core::notation::iccs::decode_move` (Local) or `decode_move_from_view` (Net) — the latter operates on a `&PlayerView` and shares its parser internals with the former. Submit on illegal/bad notation keeps the prompt open with an error in `last_msg`; Submit on a valid move clears the prompt and either calls `apply_move` (Local) or sends `ClientMsg::Move` (Net). Live-preview's cursor jump uses `orient::project_cell`, the long-marked `#[allow(dead_code)]` helper now finally pulls its weight.

## 12. Tests to add

### 12a. `chess-core/src/notation/iccs.rs` — view-flavored decoder

```rust
#[test]
fn decode_move_from_view_round_trips_step() {
    use crate::view::PlayerView;
    let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
    let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
    let m = decode_move_from_view(&view, "h2e2").unwrap();
    let m_state = decode_move(&state, "h2e2").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(m, m_state);
}

#[test]
fn decode_move_from_view_flip_in_banqi() {
    use crate::view::PlayerView;
    use crate::rules::HouseRules;
    let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 3));
    let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
    let m = decode_move_from_view(&view, "flip a0").unwrap();
    assert!(matches!(m, Move::Reveal { revealed: None, .. }));
}
```

### 12b. New `clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs` `mod tests` (none exist yet)

Use a small constructor helper since `AppState::new_game` requires a `Style`.

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use crate::glyph::Style;

    fn fresh_xiangqi() -> AppState {
        AppState::new_game(RuleSet::xiangqi(), Style::default(), false, Side::RED)
    }

    #[test]
    fn coord_instant_commits_on_enter() {
        let mut app = fresh_xiangqi();
        app.dispatch(Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Instant));
        for c in "h2e2".chars() { app.dispatch(Action::TextInput(c)); }
        app.dispatch(Action::Submit);
        if let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen {
            assert_eq!(g.state.history.len(), 1);
            assert!(g.coord_input.is_none());
        } else { panic!() }
    }

    #[test]
    fn coord_live_sets_selected_at_two_chars() {
        let mut app = fresh_xiangqi();
        app.dispatch(Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Live));
        app.dispatch(Action::TextInput('h'));
        app.dispatch(Action::TextInput('2'));
        if let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen {
            assert!(g.selected.is_some());
            // h2 = file 7, rank 2; for xiangqi 9-wide that's index 2*9 + 7 = 25.
            assert_eq!(g.selected.unwrap().0, 25);
        } else { panic!() }
    }

    #[test]
    fn coord_live_jumps_cursor_at_four_chars() {
        let mut app = fresh_xiangqi();
        let prev_cursor = if let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen { g.cursor } else { unreachable!() };
        app.dispatch(Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Live));
        for c in "h2e2".chars() { app.dispatch(Action::TextInput(c)); }
        if let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen {
            // Cursor should now sit on display(e2) for Red observer.
            // e2 = file 4, rank 2; project_cell(Red, 9x10) = (h-1-r, f) = (7, 4).
            assert_eq!(g.cursor, (7, 4));
            assert_ne!(g.cursor, prev_cursor);
        } else { panic!() }
    }

    #[test]
    fn coord_live_esc_restores_snapshot() {
        let mut app = fresh_xiangqi();
        // Move the cursor and select a piece manually first.
        if let Screen::Game(g) = &mut app.screen { g.cursor = (5, 3); }
        app.dispatch(Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Live));
        for c in "h2e2".chars() { app.dispatch(Action::TextInput(c)); }
        app.dispatch(Action::Back); // Esc
        if let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen {
            assert_eq!(g.cursor, (5, 3));
            assert!(g.selected.is_none());
            assert!(g.coord_input.is_none());
        } else { panic!() }
    }

    #[test]
    fn coord_bad_notation_keeps_prompt_open() {
        let mut app = fresh_xiangqi();
        app.dispatch(Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Instant));
        for c in "z9z9".chars() { app.dispatch(Action::TextInput(c)); }
        app.dispatch(Action::Submit);
        if let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen {
            assert_eq!(g.state.history.len(), 0);
            assert!(g.coord_input.is_some());
            assert!(g.last_msg.as_ref().unwrap().starts_with("Bad move:"));
        } else { panic!() }
    }
}
```

(Net-side tests would need a NetClient mock; defer to a follow-up since `NetView` ownership of a real `NetClient` makes the harness expensive. The Net dispatcher reuses the same shared helpers, so the Local tests cover the state-machine behavior.)

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-05-07 07:10:00Z)**_

Design an implementation plan for adding a coordinate-based move-input mode to `clients/chess-tui/`. The existing TUI only supports cursor-based moves (hjkl/arrows + Enter); we want the user to be able to type ICCS notation like `h2e2`, `flip a0`, or `a3xb3xc3` directly.

## User-decided requirements (confirmed)

1. **Two trigger keys**, both in `InputMode::Game`:
   - `:` (colon) → **Instant prompt mode** — buffer is text-only, no board highlighting; Enter commits, Esc cancels.
   - `m` → **Live preview mode** — every keystroke re-parses the buffer; if the prefix is a valid square (e.g. `h2`), set `selected = Some(square)` so the existing selected-square highlight kicks in; if a full 4-char move is typed (e.g. `h2e2`), also move the board cursor to the destination square so the existing cursor-highlight previews the target. Enter commits, Esc cancels and restores the cursor + selected state from before the user pressed `m`.

2. **Scope: both Local Game and Net play.** Spectators in Net mode get the existing-style "Spectators cannot move." message and the mode never opens.

3. **UI surface: bottom of the sidebar, yellow line** — same idiom as the existing chat-input prompt in Net mode (template at `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs:1109-1123`). Render as `> h2e2_` while typing; replace the regular hint when active.

When both `chat_input` and `coord_input` could be active in Net, treat them as mutually exclusive (entering one closes/blocks the other) — `dispatch_chat_start` and the coord-start handler check the other is `None`.

Reuse verbatim. The `parse_square` helper in iccs.rs only needs `Board` width/height, which `BoardShape` exposes. Confirm by reading `chess-core/src/notation/iccs.rs:23-39` and `chess-core/src/board.rs` for `Board::width/height`.
- **Live preview state machine** for `m`:
  - On entry: snapshot `(prev_cursor, prev_selected)`; clear `selected` and start with empty buffer.
  - On each `TextInput(c)` / `TextBackspace`: re-parse the buffer.

    - 2 chars valid square `<file><rank>`: `selected = Some(parsed_origin)`.
    - 3 chars: same as 2 (last char ignored for highlight; origin still highlighted).
    - 4 chars valid `from+to`: `selected = origin`; move `cursor` to display-coords of `to`-square via `orient::display_at_square` (note: this helper may not exist — check `clients/chess-tui/src/orient.rs` and add the inverse of `square_at_display` if missing; the existing forward function is at `orient.rs:44`).

- **Instant mode (`:`)**: same buffer + commit logic but no per-keystroke preview hook. No snapshot needed (cursor + selected untouched).

1	//! Keyboard / mouse events → `Action`. Pure mapping; no state mutation.
2	
3	use crossterm::event::{KeyCode, KeyEvent, KeyModifiers, MouseButton, MouseEvent, MouseEventKind};
4	
5	/// Where the user's focus is right now. Drives the keymap branch in
6	/// [`from_key`]. `Game` covers both local hot-seat (`Screen::Game`) and
7	/// online (`Screen::Net`) play.
8	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
9	pub enum InputMode {
10	    Picker,
11	    Lobby,
12	    Text,
13	    Game,
14	}
15	
16	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
17	pub enum Action {
18	    None,
19	    Quit,
20	    /// Confirm an open quit-confirm dialog (user pressed y/Y).
21	    ConfirmYes,
22	    /// Cancel an open quit-confirm dialog (user pressed anything else).
23	    ConfirmNo,
24	    HelpToggle,
25	    RulesToggle,
26	    NewGame,
27	    /// "Back one screen" — Esc semantics outside game/picker. The
28	    /// dispatcher decides what to back into per screen.
29	    Back,
30	    // Picker / Lobby (list-cursor)
31	    PickerUp,
32	    PickerDown,
33	    PickerSelect,
34	    // Lobby
35	    LobbyCreate,
36	    LobbyRefresh,
37	    /// Spectate the highlighted room (`?role=spectator`).
38	    LobbyWatch,
39	    // Text input (HostPrompt / CreateRoom)
40	    TextInput(char),
41	    TextBackspace,
42	    FocusNext,
43	    FocusPrev,
44	    /// Submit a text-input form (Enter while in `Text` mode).
45	    Submit,
46	    // Game
47	    CursorUp,
48	    CursorDown,
49	    CursorLeft,
50	    CursorRight,
51	    SelectOrCommit,
52	    Cancel,
53	    Undo,
54	    Flip,
55	    /// Open the chat input editor (Net mode, players only).
56	    ChatStart,
57	    /// Mouse click in terminal coords. The app resolves it against the board
58	    /// rect captured by the most recent draw().
59	    Click {
60	        term_col: u16,
61	        term_row: u16,
62	    },
63	}
64	
65	pub fn from_key(ev: KeyEvent, mode: InputMode, quit_confirm_open: bool) -> Action {
66	    // Quit-confirm dialog hijacks input: y/Y confirms, anything else cancels.
67	    if quit_confirm_open {
68	        return match ev.code {
69	            KeyCode::Char('y') | KeyCode::Char('Y') => Action::ConfirmYes,
70	            _ => Action::ConfirmNo,
71	        };
72	    }
73	    if ev.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) && matches!(ev.code, KeyCode::Char('c')) {
74	        return Action::Quit;
75	    }
76	    match mode {
77	        InputMode::Picker => match ev.code {
78	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::PickerUp,
79	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::PickerDown,
80	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::PickerSelect,
81	            KeyCode::Char('q') => Action::Quit,
82	            KeyCode::Char('?') => Action::HelpToggle,
83	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Quit,
84	            _ => Action::None,
85	        },
86	        InputMode::Lobby => match ev.code {
87	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::PickerUp,
88	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::PickerDown,
89	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::PickerSelect,
90	            KeyCode::Char('c') => Action::LobbyCreate,
91	            KeyCode::Char('r') => Action::LobbyRefresh,
92	            KeyCode::Char('w') => Action::LobbyWatch,
93	            KeyCode::Char('q') => Action::Quit,
94	            KeyCode::Char('?') => Action::HelpToggle,
95	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Back,
96	            _ => Action::None,
97	        },
98	        InputMode::Text => match ev.code {
99	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Back,
100	            KeyCode::Enter => Action::Submit,
101	            KeyCode::Backspace => Action::TextBackspace,
102	            KeyCode::Tab => Action::FocusNext,
103	            KeyCode::BackTab => Action::FocusPrev,
104	            KeyCode::Char(c) => Action::TextInput(c),
105	            _ => Action::None,
106	        },
107	        InputMode::Game => match ev.code {
108	            KeyCode::Char('q') => Action::Quit,
109	            KeyCode::Char('?') => Action::HelpToggle,
110	            KeyCode::Char('r') => Action::RulesToggle,
111	            KeyCode::Up | KeyCode::Char('k') => Action::CursorUp,
112	            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => Action::CursorDown,
113	            KeyCode::Left | KeyCode::Char('h') => Action::CursorLeft,
114	            KeyCode::Right | KeyCode::Char('l') => Action::CursorRight,
115	            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char(' ') => Action::SelectOrCommit,
116	            KeyCode::Esc => Action::Cancel,
117	            KeyCode::Char('u') => Action::Undo,
118	            KeyCode::Char('f') => Action::Flip,
119	            KeyCode::Char('n') => Action::NewGame,
120	            KeyCode::Char('t') => Action::ChatStart,
121	            _ => Action::None,
122	        },
123	    }
124	}
125	
126	pub fn from_mouse(ev: MouseEvent) -> Action {
127	    match ev.kind {
128	        MouseEventKind::Down(MouseButton::Left) => {
129	            Action::Click { term_col: ev.column, term_row: ev.row }
130	        }
131	        _ => Action::None,
132	    }
133	}
134
```

```text
1	//! ICCS notation: file `a..i` × rank `0..9` → `h2e2` style.
2	//!
3	//! Encoder is straightforward; decoder takes a state because string-form
4	//! moves are ambiguous between Step/Capture/CannonJump (the geometry is
5	//! identical from outside) — we resolve by intersecting with `legal_moves`.
6	
7	use crate::board::Board;
8	use crate::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
9	use crate::error::CoreError;
10	use crate::moves::Move;
11	use crate::state::GameState;
12	
13	/// Encode a single square as `<file><rank>` e.g. `h2`.
14	pub fn encode_square(board: &Board, sq: Square) -> String {
15	    let (f, r) = board.file_rank(sq);
16	    format!("{}{}", file_char(f.0), r.0)
17	}
18	
19	fn file_char(f: u8) -> char {
20	    (b'a' + f) as char
21	}
22	
23	fn parse_square(board: &Board, s: &str) -> Result<Square, CoreError> {
24	    let mut chars = s.chars();
25	    let f = chars.next().ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))?;
26	    let r = chars.next().ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))?;
27	    if chars.next().is_some() {
28	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()));
29	    }
30	    if !f.is_ascii_lowercase() {
31	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()));
32	    }
33	    let file_idx = (f as u8) - b'a';
34	    let rank_idx = r.to_digit(10).ok_or_else(|| CoreError::BadNotation(s.into()))? as u8;
35	    if file_idx >= board.width() || rank_idx >= board.height() {
36	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(format!("{s} out of board bounds")));
37	    }
38	    Ok(board.sq(File(file_idx), Rank(rank_idx)))
39	}
40	
41	/// Encode a move as ICCS. `Reveal` becomes `flip <sq>`. `ChainCapture`
42	/// chains squares with `x` separators.
43	pub fn encode_move(board: &Board, m: &Move) -> String {
44	    match m {
45	        Move::Reveal { at, .. } => format!("flip {}", encode_square(board, *at)),
46	        Move::Step { from, to } => {
47	            format!("{}{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
48	        }
49	        Move::Capture { from, to, .. } | Move::CannonJump { from, to, .. } => {
50	            format!("{}x{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
51	        }
52	        Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
53	            let mut s = encode_square(board, *from);
54	            for hop in path {
55	                s.push('x');
56	                s.push_str(&encode_square(board, hop.to));
57	            }
58	            s
59	        }
60	    }
61	}
62	
63	/// Decode a move from ICCS, resolving Step/Capture ambiguity by matching
64	/// against the current legal-move list. Forms accepted:
65	/// - `h2e2`               — step
66	/// - `h2xh9`              — capture (single)
67	/// - `a3xb3xc3`           — chain capture (any number of x-separated squares)
68	/// - `flip a3` / `flip a3` — reveal
69	pub fn decode_move(state: &GameState, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
70	    let trimmed = input.trim();
71	    if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("flip ") {
72	        let at = parse_square(&state.board, rest.trim())?;
73	        return Ok(Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None });
74	    }
75	    let board = &state.board;
76	
77	    // Split into squares: handle both 'h2e2' (no separator) and 'h2xe2'.
78	    let parts: Vec<Square> = if trimmed.contains('x') {
79	        trimmed.split('x').map(|p| parse_square(board, p.trim())).collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?
80	    } else if trimmed.len() == 4 {
81	        vec![parse_square(board, &trimmed[..2])?, parse_square(board, &trimmed[2..])?]
82	    } else {
83	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(input.into()));
84	    };
85	
86	    if parts.len() < 2 {
87	        return Err(CoreError::BadNotation(input.into()));
88	    }
89	
90	    let from = parts[0];
91	    let legal = state.legal_moves();
92	
93	    if parts.len() == 2 {
94	        // Step / Capture / CannonJump — find unique match by (from, to).
95	        let to = parts[1];
96	        let candidates: Vec<&Move> = legal
97	            .iter()
98	            .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(to))
99	            .collect();
100	        match candidates.as_slice() {
101	            [one] => Ok((*one).clone()),
102	            [] => Err(CoreError::Illegal("no legal move matches notation")),
103	            _multi => {
104	                // Multiple matches (e.g. Step vs ChainCapture with len 2). Prefer
105	                // the simplest: Step > Capture > CannonJump > ChainCapture.
106	                let pick = candidates
107	                    .iter()
108	                    .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. }))
109	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Capture { .. })))
110	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::CannonJump { .. })))
111	                    .or_else(|| candidates.iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::ChainCapture { .. })));
112	                pick.map(|m| (*m).clone()).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("ambiguous"))
113	            }
114	        }
115	    } else {
116	        // ChainCapture — find legal move whose path matches.
117	        let chain_squares = &parts[1..];
118	        let candidate = legal.iter().find(|m| match m {
119	            Move::ChainCapture { from: f, path } => {
120	                *f == from
121	                    && path.len() == chain_squares.len()
122	                    && path.iter().zip(chain_squares.iter()).all(|(h, sq)| h.to == *sq)
123	            }
124	            _ => false,
125	        });
126	        candidate.cloned().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no chain capture matches notation"))
127	    }
128	}
129	
130	#[cfg(test)]
131	mod tests {
132	    use super::*;
133	    use crate::rules::RuleSet;
134	
135	    #[test]
136	    fn round_trip_simple_step() {
137	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
138	        // Pick a legal Step and round-trip it.
139	        let m = state.legal_moves().into_iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. })).unwrap();
140	        let s = encode_move(&state.board, &m);
141	        let decoded = decode_move(&state, &s).unwrap();
142	        assert_eq!(m, decoded);
143	    }
144	
145	    #[test]
146	    fn parse_h2e2_in_xiangqi() {
147	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
148	        // h2 is a Red cannon. h2e2 moves it 6 squares west — the standard 炮二平五 (well, 炮 to e2).
149	        let m = decode_move(&state, "h2e2").unwrap();
150	        if let Move::Step { from, to } = m {
151	            let (f1, r1) = state.board.file_rank(from);
152	            let (f2, r2) = state.board.file_rank(to);
153	            assert_eq!((f1.0, r1.0, f2.0, r2.0), (7, 2, 4, 2));
154	        } else {
155	            panic!("expected Step, got {m:?}");
156	        }
157	    }

159	    #[test]
160	    fn flip_decodes_to_reveal() {
161	        let state = GameState::new(crate::rules::RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(
162	            crate::rules::HouseRules::empty(),
163	            3,
164	        ));
165	        let m = decode_move(&state, "flip a0").unwrap();
166	        assert!(matches!(m, Move::Reveal { revealed: None, .. }));
167	    }

171	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
172	        assert!(decode_move(&state, "").is_err());
173	        assert!(decode_move(&state, "zz").is_err());
174	        assert!(decode_move(&state, "z9z9").is_err());
