```text
334	    /// Drain ws events from the worker thread(s) and apply them to the
335	    /// active `NetView` / `LobbyView`. Called once per main-loop tick
336	    /// (no-op outside Net / Lobby modes).
337	    pub fn tick_net(&mut self) {
338	        match &mut self.screen {
339	            Screen::Net(n) => {
340	                while let Ok(evt) = n.client.evt_rx.try_recv() {
341	                    apply_net_event(n, evt);
342	                }
343	            }
344	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
345	                while let Ok(evt) = l.client.evt_rx.try_recv() {
346	                    apply_lobby_event(l, evt);
347	                }
348	            }
349	            _ => {}
350	        }
351	    }
352	
353	    /// Compute the input mode for the current screen so main.rs can drive
354	    /// `from_key` without poking at private state.
355	    pub fn input_mode(&self) -> InputMode {
356	        match &self.screen {
357	            Screen::Picker(_) => InputMode::Picker,
358	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
359	                if l.pending_join.is_some() {
360	                    InputMode::Text
361	                } else {
362	                    InputMode::Lobby
363	                }
364	            }
365	            Screen::HostPrompt(_) | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => InputMode::Text,
366	            // While typing a chat line, hijack the keymap so printable chars
367	            // append to the buffer instead of moving the cursor.
368	            Screen::Net(n) if n.chat_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
369	            Screen::Game(_) | Screen::Net(_) => InputMode::Game,
370	        }
371	    }
372	
373	    pub fn dispatch(&mut self, action: Action) {
374	        match action {
375	            Action::None => {}
376	            Action::ConfirmYes => {
377	                self.quit_confirm_open = false;
378	                self.should_quit = true;
379	            }
380	            Action::ConfirmNo => self.quit_confirm_open = false,
381	            Action::Quit => {
382	                if self.is_game_in_progress() {
383	                    self.quit_confirm_open = true;
384	                } else {
385	                    self.should_quit = true;
386	                }
387	            }
388	            Action::HelpToggle => self.help_open = !self.help_open,
389	            Action::RulesToggle => self.rules_open = !self.rules_open,
390	            Action::NewGame => {
391	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Net(_)) {
392	                    // In Net mode, 'n' requests a rematch via the server
393	                    // instead of dropping the connection. Game must be over.
394	                    if let Screen::Net(n) = &mut self.screen {
395	                        if !n.role.map(|r| r.is_player()).unwrap_or(false) {
396	                            n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot request a rematch.".into());
397	                            return;
398	                        }
399	                        let status = n.last_view.as_ref().map(|v| v.status);
400	                        match status {
401	                            Some(GameStatus::Won { .. }) | Some(GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) => {
402	                                let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Rematch);
403	                                n.last_msg =
404	                                    Some("Rematch requested. Waiting for opponent…".into());
405	                            }
406	                            Some(GameStatus::Ongoing) => {
407	                                n.last_msg = Some(
408	                                    "'n' requests a rematch only after the game is over.".into(),
409	                                );
410	                            }
411	                            None => {
412	                                n.last_msg = Some("Not connected yet.".into());
413	                            }
414	                        }
415	                    }
416	                } else {
417	                    let style = self.style;
418	                    let use_color = self.use_color;
419	                    let observer = self.observer;
420	                    *self = AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer);
421	                }
422	            }
423	            Action::Back => self.dispatch_back(),
424	            Action::PickerUp | Action::PickerDown | Action::PickerSelect => match &self.screen {
425	                Screen::Picker(_) => self.dispatch_picker(action),
426	                Screen::Lobby(_) => self.dispatch_lobby(action),
427	                _ => {}
428	            },
429	            Action::LobbyCreate | Action::LobbyRefresh => {
430	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Lobby(_)) {
431	                    self.dispatch_lobby(action);
432	                }
433	            }
434	            Action::TextInput(_)
435	            | Action::TextBackspace
436	            | Action::FocusNext
437	            | Action::FocusPrev
438	            | Action::Submit => self.dispatch_text(action),
439	            Action::ChatStart => self.dispatch_chat_start(),
440	            Action::LobbyWatch => {
441	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Lobby(_)) {
442	                    self.dispatch_lobby_watch();
443	                }
444	            }
445	            _ => match &self.screen {
446	                Screen::Net(_) => self.dispatch_net(action),
447	                Screen::Game(_) => self.dispatch_game(action),
448	                Screen::Picker(_)
449	                | Screen::HostPrompt(_)
450	                | Screen::Lobby(_)
451	                | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => {}
452	            },
453	        }
454	    }
455	
456	    fn dispatch_back(&mut self) {
457	        let style = self.style;
458	        let use_color = self.use_color;
459	        let observer = self.observer;
460	        match &mut self.screen {
461	            Screen::HostPrompt(_) => {
462	                *self = AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer);
463	            }
464	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
465	                if l.pending_join.is_some() {
466	                    l.pending_join = None;
467	                    l.last_msg = None;
468	                    return;
469	                }
470	                *self = AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer);
471	            }
472	            Screen::CreateRoom(c) => {
473	                let host = c.host.clone();
474	                *self = AppState::new_lobby(host, style, use_color, observer);
475	            }
476	            // Esc inside Net mode — if we're typing a chat line, cancel the
477	            // input rather than navigating away.
478	            Screen::Net(n) if n.chat_input.is_some() => {
479	                n.chat_input = None;
480	                n.last_msg = None;
481	            }
482	            _ => {}
483	        }
484	    }
485	
486	    fn dispatch_chat_start(&mut self) {
487	        let Screen::Net(n) = &mut self.screen else {
488	            return;
489	        };
490	        match n.role {
491	            Some(NetRole::Player(_)) => {
492	                n.chat_input = Some(String::new());
493	                n.last_msg = Some("Chat: type a line, Enter sends, Esc cancels.".into());
494	            }
495	            Some(NetRole::Spectator) => {
496	                n.last_msg = Some("Spectators can read but not chat.".into());
497	            }
498	            None => {
499	                n.last_msg = Some("Not connected yet.".into());
500	            }
501	        }
502	    }
503	
504	    fn dispatch_lobby_watch(&mut self) {
505	        let Screen::Lobby(l) = &mut self.screen else {
506	            return;
507	        };
508	        if l.pending_join.is_some() || l.rooms.is_empty() {
509	            return;
510	        }
511	        let cursor = l.cursor.min(l.rooms.len() - 1);
512	        let room = l.rooms[cursor].clone();
513	        if room.has_password {
514	            // Spectator joins to locked rooms still need the password.
515	            l.pending_join = Some(PendingJoin {
516	                room_id: room.id,
517	                password_buf: String::new(),
518	                as_spectator: true,
519	            });
520	            l.last_msg = Some("Type password to spectate, Enter to join, Esc to cancel.".into());
521	            return;
522	        }
523	        let host = l.host.clone();
524	        let style = self.style;
525	        let use_color = self.use_color;
526	        let url = format!("{host}/ws/{}?role=spectator", room.id);
527	        *self = AppState::new_net(url, style, use_color);
528	    }
529	
530	    fn is_game_in_progress(&self) -> bool {
531	        match &self.screen {
532	            Screen::Game(g) => {
533	                matches!(g.state.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) && !g.state.history.is_empty()
534	            }
535	            Screen::Net(n) => match &n.last_view {
536	                Some(view) => matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) && n.connected,
537	                None => false,
538	            },
539	            Screen::Picker(_)
540	            | Screen::HostPrompt(_)
541	            | Screen::Lobby(_)
542	            | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => false,
543	        }
544	    }
545	
546	    fn dispatch_picker(&mut self, action: Action) {
547	        let Screen::Picker(p) = &mut self.screen else {
548	            return;
549	        };
550	        let n = PickerEntry::ALL.len();
551	        match action {
552	            Action::PickerUp => p.cursor = (p.cursor + n - 1) % n,
553	            Action::PickerDown => p.cursor = (p.cursor + 1) % n,
554	            Action::PickerSelect => {
555	                let entry = PickerEntry::ALL[p.cursor];
556	                let observer = self.observer;
557	                let style = self.style;
558	                let use_color = self.use_color;
559	                match entry {
560	                    PickerEntry::ConnectToServer => {
561	                        *self = AppState::new_host_prompt(style, use_color, observer);
562	                    }
563	                    PickerEntry::Quit => self.should_quit = true,
564	                    other => {
565	                        if let Some(rules) = other.rules() {
566	                            *self = AppState::new_game(rules, style, use_color, observer);
567	                        }
568	                    }
569	                }
570	            }
571	            _ => {}
572	        }
573	    }
574	
575	    fn dispatch_lobby(&mut self, action: Action) {
576	        let Screen::Lobby(l) = &mut self.screen else {
577	            return;
578	        };
579	        // Pending password prompt eats list-cursor inputs.
580	        if l.pending_join.is_some() {
581	            return;
582	        }
583	        match action {
584	            Action::PickerUp if !l.rooms.is_empty() => {
585	                l.cursor = (l.cursor + l.rooms.len() - 1) % l.rooms.len();
586	            }
587	            Action::PickerDown if !l.rooms.is_empty() => {
588	                l.cursor = (l.cursor + 1) % l.rooms.len();
589	            }
590	            Action::PickerSelect => {
591	                if l.rooms.is_empty() {
592	                    l.last_msg =
593	                        Some("No rooms yet. Press 'c' to create one, or 'r' to refresh.".into());
594	                    return;
595	                }
596	                let cursor = l.cursor.min(l.rooms.len() - 1);
597	                let room = l.rooms[cursor].clone();
598	                if room.seats >= 2 {
599	                    l.last_msg = Some(format!("Room '{}' is full (2/2).", room.id));
600	                    return;
601	                }
602	                if room.has_password {
603	                    l.pending_join = Some(PendingJoin {
604	                        room_id: room.id,
605	                        password_buf: String::new(),
606	                        as_spectator: false,
607	                    });
608	                    l.last_msg = Some("Type password, Enter to join, Esc to cancel.".into());
609	                    return;
610	                }
611	                let host = l.host.clone();
612	                let style = self.style;
613	                let use_color = self.use_color;
614	                *self = AppState::new_net(format!("{host}/ws/{}", room.id), style, use_color);
615	            }
616	            Action::LobbyCreate => {
617	                let host = l.host.clone();
618	                self.screen = Screen::CreateRoom(CreateRoomView {
619	                    host,
620	                    id_buf: String::new(),
621	                    password_buf: String::new(),
622	                    focus: CreateRoomField::Id,
623	                    error: None,
624	                });
625	            }
626	            Action::LobbyRefresh => {
627	                let _ = l.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::ListRooms);
628	                l.last_msg = Some("Refresh requested.".into());
629	            }
630	            _ => {}
631	        }
632	    }
633	
634	    fn dispatch_text(&mut self, action: Action) {
635	        match &mut self.screen {
636	            Screen::HostPrompt(h) => match action {
637	                Action::TextInput(c) => text_input::push_char(&mut h.buf, c, 128),
638	                Action::TextBackspace => text_input::backspace(&mut h.buf),
639	                Action::Submit => {
640	                    let raw = h.buf.trim().to_string();
641	                    let host = match normalize_host_url(&raw) {
642	                        Ok(u) => u,
643	                        Err(e) => {
644	                            h.error = Some(e);
645	                            return;
646	                        }
647	                    };
648	                    let style = self.style;
649	                    let use_color = self.use_color;
650	                    let observer = self.observer;
651	                    *self = AppState::new_lobby(host, style, use_color, observer);
652	                }
653	                _ => {}
654	            },
655	            Screen::CreateRoom(c) => match action {
656	                Action::TextInput(ch) => match c.focus {
657	                    CreateRoomField::Id => text_input::push_char(&mut c.id_buf, ch, 32),
658	                    CreateRoomField::Password => text_input::push_char(&mut c.password_buf, ch, 64),
659	                    CreateRoomField::Submit => {
660	                        if matches!(ch, ' ' | '\n') {
661	                            self.dispatch_text(Action::Submit);
662	                        }
663	                    }
664	                },
665	                Action::TextBackspace => match c.focus {
666	                    CreateRoomField::Id => text_input::backspace(&mut c.id_buf),
667	                    CreateRoomField::Password => text_input::backspace(&mut c.password_buf),
668	                    _ => {}
669	                },
670	                Action::FocusNext => {
671	                    c.focus = match c.focus {
672	                        CreateRoomField::Id => CreateRoomField::Password,
673	                        CreateRoomField::Password => CreateRoomField::Submit,
674	                        CreateRoomField::Submit => CreateRoomField::Id,
675	                    };
676	                }
677	                Action::FocusPrev => {
678	                    c.focus = match c.focus {
679	                        CreateRoomField::Id => CreateRoomField::Submit,
680	                        CreateRoomField::Password => CreateRoomField::Id,
681	                        CreateRoomField::Submit => CreateRoomField::Password,
682	                    };
683	                }
684	                Action::Submit => {
685	                    let id = c.id_buf.trim().to_string();
686	                    if !valid_room_id(&id) {
687	                        c.error = Some("Room id must be 1–32 chars of [a-zA-Z0-9_-].".into());
688	                        return;
689	                    }
690	                    let host = c.host.clone();
691	                    let password =
692	                        if c.password_buf.is_empty() { None } else { Some(c.password_buf.clone()) };
693	                    let url = match password {
694	                        Some(pw) => format!("{host}/ws/{id}?password={}", urlencode(&pw)),
695	                        None => format!("{host}/ws/{id}"),
696	                    };
697	                    let style = self.style;
698	                    let use_color = self.use_color;
699	                    *self = AppState::new_net(url, style, use_color);
700	                }
701	                _ => {}
702	            },
703	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
704	                let Some(pj) = l.pending_join.as_mut() else {
705	                    return;
706	                };
707	                match action {
708	                    Action::TextInput(c) => text_input::push_char(&mut pj.password_buf, c, 64),
709	                    Action::TextBackspace => text_input::backspace(&mut pj.password_buf),
710	                    Action::Submit => {
711	                        let host = l.host.clone();
712	                        let pj_owned = l.pending_join.take().unwrap();
713	                        let mut url = format!(
714	                            "{host}/ws/{}?password={}",
715	                            pj_owned.room_id,
716	                            urlencode(&pj_owned.password_buf)
717	                        );
718	                        if pj_owned.as_spectator {
719	                            url.push_str("&role=spectator");
720	                        }
721	                        let style = self.style;
722	                        let use_color = self.use_color;
723	                        *self = AppState::new_net(url, style, use_color);
724	                    }
725	                    _ => {}
726	                }
727	            }
728	            // Net mode hijacks Text input while the user is composing a chat
729	            // line. Submit fires Chat over the wire; Esc / Back cancels (see
730	            // dispatch_back).
731	            Screen::Net(n) => {
732	                let Some(buf) = n.chat_input.as_mut() else {
733	                    return;
734	                };
735	                match action {
736	                    Action::TextInput(c) => text_input::push_char(buf, c, CHAT_INPUT_MAX),
737	                    Action::TextBackspace => text_input::backspace(buf),
738	                    Action::Submit => {
739	                        let buf = n.chat_input.take().unwrap_or_default();
740	                        let trimmed = buf.trim();
741	                        if trimmed.is_empty() {
742	                            n.last_msg = Some("Empty chat — nothing sent.".into());
743	                            return;
744	                        }
745	                        let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Chat { text: trimmed.to_string() });
746	                        n.last_msg = Some("Chat sent.".into());
747	                    }
748	                    _ => {}
749	                }
750	            }
751	            _ => {}
752	        }
753	    }
754	
755	    fn dispatch_game(&mut self, action: Action) {
756	        let Screen::Game(g) = &mut self.screen else {
757	            return;
758	        };
759	        let shape = g.state.board.shape();
760	        let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
761	        match action {
762	            Action::CursorUp if g.cursor.0 > 0 => {
763	                g.cursor.0 -= 1;
764	            }
765	            Action::CursorDown if g.cursor.0 + 1 < rows => {
766	                g.cursor.0 += 1;
767	            }
768	            Action::CursorLeft if g.cursor.1 > 0 => {
769	                g.cursor.1 -= 1;
770	            }
771	            Action::CursorRight if g.cursor.1 + 1 < cols => {
772	                g.cursor.1 += 1;
773	            }
774	            Action::Cancel => {
775	                g.selected = None;
776	                g.last_msg = None;
777	            }
778	            Action::SelectOrCommit => {
779	                let observer = self.observer;
780	                Self::handle_select_or_commit(g, observer);
781	            }
782	            Action::Undo => match g.state.unmake_move() {
783	                Ok(()) => {
784	                    g.state.refresh_status();
785	                    g.selected = None;
786	                    g.last_msg = Some(format!("Undone. {:?} to move.", g.state.side_to_move));
787	                }
788	                Err(e) => {
789	                    g.last_msg = Some(format!("Cannot undo: {e}"));
790	                }
791	            },
792	            Action::Flip => {
793	                if !matches!(g.state.status, chess_core::state::GameStatus::Ongoing) {
794	                    g.last_msg = Some(
795	                        "Game over. Press 'n' for new game, 'u' to take back, 'q' to quit.".into(),
796	                    );
797	                    return;
798	                }
799	                let observer = self.observer;
800	                let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(g.cursor.0, g.cursor.1, observer, shape)
801	                else {
802	                    g.last_msg = Some("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
803	                    return;
804	                };
805	                let m = Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None };
806	                Self::apply_move(g, m);
807	            }
808	            Action::Click { term_col, term_row } => {
809	                if let Some(rect) = self.board_rect {
810	                    let observer = self.observer;
811	                    if let Some((row, col)) = hit_test(rect, term_col, term_row, rows, cols) {
812	                        g.cursor = (row, col);
813	                        Self::handle_select_or_commit(g, observer);
814	                    }
815	                }
816	            }
817	            _ => {}
818	        }
819	    }
820	
821	    fn handle_select_or_commit(g: &mut GameView, observer: Side) {
822	        if !matches!(g.state.status, chess_core::state::GameStatus::Ongoing) {
823	            g.last_msg =
824	                Some("Game over. Press 'n' for new game, 'u' to take back, 'q' to quit.".into());
825	            return;
826	        }
827	        let shape = g.state.board.shape();
828	        let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(g.cursor.0, g.cursor.1, observer, shape) else {
829	            g.last_msg = Some("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
830	            return;
831	        };
832	
833	        let view = PlayerView::project(&g.state, g.state.side_to_move);
834	
835	        match g.selected {
836	            None => {
837	                // Try to select a piece. Allowed if there's a legal move from this square.
838	                let any = view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq);
839	                if any {
840	                    g.selected = Some(sq);
841	                    g.last_msg = None;
842	                } else {
843	                    // Maybe it's a banqi hidden cell — suggest 'f' to flip.
844	                    match g.state.board.get(sq) {
845	                        Some(p) if !p.revealed => {
846	                            g.last_msg = Some("Hidden piece. Press 'f' to flip.".into());
847	                        }
848	                        _ => g.last_msg = Some("No legal move from that square.".into()),
849	                    }
850	                }
851	            }
852	            Some(from) if from == sq => {
853	                g.selected = None;
854	                g.last_msg = None;
855	            }
856	            Some(from) => {
857	                // Find a legal move from `from` to `sq`. Prefer Capture / CannonJump
858	                // over a chain ending at sq (for banqi); take the first match.
859	                let candidate = view
860	                    .legal_moves
861	                    .iter()
862	                    .find(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(sq))
863	                    .cloned();
864	                match candidate {
865	                    Some(m) => Self::apply_move(g, m),
866	                    None => {
867	                        g.last_msg = Some("Illegal move.".into());
868	                        g.selected = None;
869	                    }
870	                }
871	            }
872	        }
873	    }
874	
875	    fn apply_move(g: &mut GameView, m: Move) {
876	        match g.state.make_move(&m) {
877	            Ok(()) => {
878	                g.state.refresh_status();
879	                g.selected = None;
880	                g.last_msg = Some(format!(
881	                    "Played: {}",
882	                    chess_core::notation::iccs::encode_move(&g.state.board, &m)
883	                ));
884	            }
885	            Err(e) => {
886	                g.last_msg = Some(format!("Engine rejected move: {e}"));
887	                g.selected = None;
888	            }
889	        }
890	    }
891	
892	    fn dispatch_net(&mut self, action: Action) {
893	        let Screen::Net(n) = &mut self.screen else {
894	            return;
895	        };
896	        // Pre-welcome: no view yet, nothing to dispatch on.
897	        let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else {
898	            return;
899	        };
900	        let shape = view.shape;
901	        let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
902	        let role = n.role.unwrap_or(NetRole::Player(Side::RED));
903	        let observer = role.observer();
904	        match action {
905	            Action::CursorUp if n.cursor.0 > 0 => {
906	                n.cursor.0 -= 1;
907	            }
908	            Action::CursorDown if n.cursor.0 + 1 < rows => {
909	                n.cursor.0 += 1;
910	            }
911	            Action::CursorLeft if n.cursor.1 > 0 => {
912	                n.cursor.1 -= 1;
913	            }
914	            Action::CursorRight if n.cursor.1 + 1 < cols => {
915	                n.cursor.1 += 1;
916	            }
917	            Action::Cancel => {
918	                n.selected = None;
919	                n.last_msg = None;
920	            }
921	            Action::SelectOrCommit => {
922	                if role.is_spectator() {
923	                    n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
924	                    return;
925	                }
926	                let outcome = compute_select_outcome(n, observer);
927	                apply_select_outcome(n, outcome);
928	            }
929	            Action::Flip => {
930	                if role.is_spectator() {
931	                    n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
932	                    return;
933	                }
934	                if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
935	                    n.last_msg = Some("Game over.".into());
936	                    return;
937	                }
938	                if view.side_to_move != observer {
939	                    n.last_msg = Some("Not your turn.".into());
940	                    return;
941	                }
942	                let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(n.cursor.0, n.cursor.1, observer, shape)
943	                else {
944	                    n.last_msg = Some("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
945	                    return;
946	                };
947	                let _ = n
948	                    .client
949	                    .cmd_tx
950	                    .send(ClientMsg::Move { mv: Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None } });
951	                n.last_msg = Some("Reveal sent.".into());
952	            }
953	            Action::Undo => {
954	                n.last_msg = Some("Undo not supported in online mode yet.".into());
955	            }
956	            Action::Click { term_col, term_row } => {
957	                if role.is_spectator() {
958	                    return;
959	                }
960	                if let Some(rect) = self.board_rect {
961	                    if let Some((row, col)) = hit_test(rect, term_col, term_row, rows, cols) {
962	                        let n = match &mut self.screen {
963	                            Screen::Net(b) => b,
964	                            _ => return,
965	                        };
966	                        n.cursor = (row, col);
967	                        let outcome = compute_select_outcome(n, observer);
968	                        apply_select_outcome(n, outcome);
969	                    }
970	                }
971	            }
972	            _ => {}
973	        }
974	    }
975	}
976	
977	enum SelectOutcome {
978	    Ignore,
979	    Msg(String),
980	    ClearAndMsg(String),
981	    Select(Square),
982	    Deselect,
983	    Commit(Move),
984	}
985	
986	fn compute_select_outcome(n: &NetView, observer: Side) -> SelectOutcome {
987	    let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else {
988	        return SelectOutcome::Ignore;
989	    };
990	    if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
991	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Game over.".into());
992	    }
993	    let shape = view.shape;
994	    let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(n.cursor.0, n.cursor.1, observer, shape) else {
995	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
996	    };
997	    if view.side_to_move != observer {
998	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Not your turn.".into());
999	    }
1000	    match n.selected {
1001	        None => {
1002	            if view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq) {
1003	                SelectOutcome::Select(sq)
1004	            } else if matches!(view.cells[sq.0 as usize], VisibleCell::Hidden) {
1005	                SelectOutcome::Msg("Hidden piece. Press 'f' to flip.".into())
1006	            } else {
1007	                SelectOutcome::Msg("No legal move from that square.".into())
1008	            }
1009	        }
1010	        Some(from) if from == sq => SelectOutcome::Deselect,
1011	        Some(from) => {
1012	            let candidate = view
1013	                .legal_moves
1014	                .iter()
1015	                .find(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(sq))
1016	                .cloned();
1017	            match candidate {
1018	                Some(mv) => SelectOutcome::Commit(mv),
1019	                None => SelectOutcome::ClearAndMsg("Illegal move.".into()),
1020	            }
1021	        }
1022	    }
1023	}
1024	
1025	fn apply_select_outcome(n: &mut NetView, outcome: SelectOutcome) {
1026	    match outcome {
1027	        SelectOutcome::Ignore => {}
1028	        SelectOutcome::Msg(m) => n.last_msg = Some(m),
1029	        SelectOutcome::ClearAndMsg(m) => {
1030	            n.selected = None;
1031	            n.last_msg = Some(m);
1032	        }
1033	        SelectOutcome::Select(sq) => {
1034	            n.selected = Some(sq);
1035	            n.last_msg = None;
1036	        }
1037	        SelectOutcome::Deselect => {
1038	            n.selected = None;
1039	            n.last_msg = None;
1040	        }
1041	        SelectOutcome::Commit(mv) => {
1042	            let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
1043	            n.selected = None;
1044	            n.last_msg = Some("Sent.".into());
1045	        }
1046	    }
1047	}
1048	
1049	fn apply_net_event(n: &mut NetView, evt: NetEvent) {
1050	    match evt {
1051	        NetEvent::Connected => {
1052	            n.connected = true;
1053	            n.last_msg = Some("Connected. Waiting for welcome…".into());
1054	        }
1055	        NetEvent::Server(boxed) => match *boxed {
1056	            ServerMsg::Hello { observer, rules, view, .. } => {
1057	                let was_seated = n.role.is_some();
1058	                n.role = Some(NetRole::Player(observer));
1059	                n.rules = Some(rules);
1060	                let shape = view.shape;
1061	                let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
1062	                n.cursor = (rows / 2, cols / 2);
1063	                n.selected = None;
1064	                n.last_view = Some(view);
1065	                n.connected = true;
1066	                // First Hello = "Joined as X". Subsequent Hello = rematch reset.
1067	                n.last_msg = Some(if was_seated {
1068	                    "Rematch — new game.".into()
1069	                } else {
1070	                    format!("Joined as {}.", side_label(observer))
1071	                });
1072	            }
1073	            ServerMsg::Spectating { rules, view, .. } => {
1074	                let was_welcomed = n.role.is_some();
1075	                n.role = Some(NetRole::Spectator);
1076	                n.rules = Some(rules);
1077	                let shape = view.shape;
1078	                let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
1079	                n.cursor = (rows / 2, cols / 2);
1080	                n.selected = None;
1081	                n.last_view = Some(view);
1082	                n.connected = true;
1083	                n.last_msg = Some(if was_welcomed {
1084	                    "Rematch — new game (spectating).".into()
1085	                } else {
1086	                    "Joined as spectator (read-only).".into()
1087	                });
1088	            }
1089	            ServerMsg::Update { view } => {
1090	                n.last_view = Some(view);
1091	                if n.last_msg.as_deref() == Some("Sent.") {
1092	                    n.last_msg = None;
1093	                }
1094	            }
1095	            ServerMsg::ChatHistory { lines } => {
1096	                n.chat.clear();
1097	                for line in lines.into_iter().take(CHAT_HISTORY_CAP) {
1098	                    n.chat.push_back(line);
1099	                }
1100	            }
1101	            ServerMsg::Chat { line } => {
1102	                n.chat.push_back(line);
1103	                while n.chat.len() > CHAT_HISTORY_CAP {
1104	                    n.chat.pop_front();
1105	                }
1106	            }
1107	            ServerMsg::Error { message } => {
1108	                n.last_msg = Some(message);
1109	            }
1110	            ServerMsg::Rooms { .. } => {
1111	                n.last_msg = Some("(unexpected lobby payload on game socket)".into());
1112	            }
1113	        },
1114	        NetEvent::Disconnected(reason) => {
1115	            n.connected = false;
1116	            n.last_msg = Some(format!("Disconnected: {reason}"));
1117	        }
1118	    }
1119	}
1120	
1121	fn side_label(side: Side) -> &'static str {
1122	    if side == Side::RED {
1123	        "Red 紅"
1124	    } else if side == Side::BLACK {
1125	        "Black 黑"
1126	    } else {
1127	        "Green 綠"
1128	    }
1129	}
1130	
1131	fn apply_lobby_event(l: &mut LobbyView, evt: NetEvent) {
1132	    match evt {
1133	        NetEvent::Connected => {
1134	            l.connected = true;
1135	            l.last_msg = Some("Lobby connected.".into());
1136	        }
1137	        NetEvent::Server(boxed) => match *boxed {
1138	            ServerMsg::Rooms { rooms } => {
1139	                let prev_id = l.rooms.get(l.cursor).map(|r| r.id.clone());
1140	                l.rooms = rooms;
1141	                l.rooms.sort_by(|a, b| a.id.cmp(&b.id));
1142	                // Try to keep the cursor on the same room id; otherwise clamp.
1143	                if let Some(id) = prev_id {
1144	                    if let Some(idx) = l.rooms.iter().position(|r| r.id == id) {
1145	                        l.cursor = idx;
1146	                    } else if l.cursor >= l.rooms.len() && !l.rooms.is_empty() {
1147	                        l.cursor = l.rooms.len() - 1;
1148	                    } else if l.rooms.is_empty() {
1149	                        l.cursor = 0;
1150	                    }
1151	                } else if l.cursor >= l.rooms.len() && !l.rooms.is_empty() {
1152	                    l.cursor = l.rooms.len() - 1;
1153	                }
1154	            }
1155	            ServerMsg::Error { message } => {
1156	                l.last_msg = Some(message);
1157	            }
1158	            ServerMsg::Hello { .. }
1159	            | ServerMsg::Update { .. }
1160	            | ServerMsg::Spectating { .. }
1161	            | ServerMsg::ChatHistory { .. }
1162	            | ServerMsg::Chat { .. } => {
1163	                // Game-socket payloads should never arrive on a lobby ws.
1164	                // If they do (server bug), surface for debugging.
1165	                l.last_msg = Some("(unexpected game payload on lobby socket)".into());
1166	            }
1167	        },
1168	        NetEvent::Disconnected(reason) => {
1169	            l.connected = false;
1170	            l.last_msg = Some(format!("Lobby disconnected: {reason}"));
1171	        }
1172	    }
1173	}
1174	
1175	/// Convert terminal click coords to (display_row, display_col) within board.
1176	///
1177	/// Cells are `cell_rows × cell_cols` terminal cells. With the intersection
1178	/// layout, `cell_rows = 2` (rank row + between row) so clicks on either of
1179	/// those rows resolve to the same display cell — including the river row,
1180	/// which simply replaces the between-row at index 4 without changing the
1181	/// row layout.
1182	fn hit_test(rect: RectPx, term_col: u16, term_row: u16, rows: u8, cols: u8) -> Option<(u8, u8)> {
1183	    if term_col < rect.x + rect.left_pad || term_row < rect.y + rect.top_pad {
1184	        return None;
1185	    }
1186	    let col_off = term_col - rect.x - rect.left_pad;
1187	    let row_off = term_row - rect.y - rect.top_pad;
1188	    if rect.cell_cols == 0 || rect.cell_rows == 0 {
1189	        return None;
1190	    }
1191	    let cell_col = col_off / rect.cell_cols;
1192	    let cell_row = row_off / rect.cell_rows;
1193	    if cell_row >= rows as u16 || cell_col >= cols as u16 {
1194	        return None;
1195	    }
1196	    Some((cell_row as u8, cell_col as u8))
1197	}
1198
```

```text
1	//! Display orientation. Engine coords are physical (Red on rank 0); the
2	//! TUI rotates / transposes per shape so each observer sees their own pieces
3	//! at the bottom of the screen, and banqi displays in traditional 8×4 layout.
4	
5	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
6	use chess_core::coord::Square;
7	use chess_core::piece::Side;
8	
9	/// Dimensions of the display grid in (rows, cols). Banqi transposes; xiangqi
10	/// keeps model dimensions as-is.
11	pub fn display_dims(shape: BoardShape) -> (u8, u8) {
12	    let (w, h) = shape.dimensions();
13	    match shape {
14	        BoardShape::Banqi4x8 => (w, h), // 4 rows × 8 cols
15	        _ => (h, w),                    // 10 rows × 9 cols for xiangqi
16	    }
17	}
18	
19	/// Square (model coord) → (display_row, display_col).
20	///
21	/// The TUI renderer iterates display coords forward and uses
22	/// `square_at_display` for the inverse, so this function is currently exercised
23	/// by tests only. It stays in the public surface as the symmetric counterpart
24	/// (and for future widgets that walk the model side, e.g. captured-piece tray).
25	#[allow(dead_code)]
26	pub fn project_cell(sq: Square, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> (u8, u8) {
27	    let (w, h) = shape.dimensions();
28	    let f = (sq.0 as u8) % w;
29	    let r = (sq.0 as u8) / w;
30	    match shape {
31	        BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10 => match observer {
32	            Side::RED => (h - 1 - r, f),   // rank 9 at top, rank 0 at bottom
33	            Side::BLACK => (r, w - 1 - f), // rank 0 at top, files reversed
34	            _ => (h - 1 - r, f),           // 三國暗棋 fallback
35	        },
36	        BoardShape::Banqi4x8 => (f, r), // transpose: rank → col, file → row
37	        BoardShape::ThreeKingdom => (h - 1 - r, f),
38	        BoardShape::Custom { .. } => (h - 1 - r, f),
39	    }
40	}
41	
42	/// Display (row, col) → Square. Returns `None` when out of bounds or the
43	/// shape masks that cell off (Custom / ThreeKingdom).
44	pub fn square_at_display(row: u8, col: u8, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> Option<Square> {
45	    let (w, h) = shape.dimensions();
46	    let (dr, dc) = display_dims(shape);
47	    if row >= dr || col >= dc {
48	        return None;
49	    }
50	    let (f, r) = match shape {
51	        BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10 => match observer {
52	            Side::RED => (col, h - 1 - row),
53	            Side::BLACK => (w - 1 - col, row),
54	            _ => (col, h - 1 - row),
55	        },
56	        BoardShape::Banqi4x8 => (row, col),
57	        BoardShape::ThreeKingdom => (col, h - 1 - row),
58	        BoardShape::Custom { .. } => (col, h - 1 - row),
59	    };
60	    if f >= w || r >= h {
61	        return None;
62	    }
63	    let idx = (r as u16) * (w as u16) + (f as u16);
64	    if !shape.is_playable(idx as usize) {
65	        return None;
66	    }
67	    Some(Square(idx))
68	}
69

76	        (0..n).map(Square).filter(move |s| shape.is_playable(s.0 as usize))
77	    }
78

93	            assert_eq!(square_at_display(r, c, Side::BLACK, shape), Some(sq));
94	        }
```

```text
1072	const MIN_META_ROWS: u16 = 14;

1074	
1075	fn draw_chat_pane(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, n: &NetView, input_active: bool) {
1076	    let block = Block::default()
1077	        .borders(Borders::TOP)
1078	        .border_style(TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray))
1079	        .title(" Chat ");
1080	    let inner = block.inner(area);
1081	    frame.render_widget(block, area);
1082	    if inner.height == 0 {
1083	        return;
1084	    }
1085	
1086	    // Reserve the bottom row for the input prompt (or hint when not typing).
1087	    let log_h = inner.height.saturating_sub(1);
1088	    let log_area = Rect { x: inner.x, y: inner.y, width: inner.width, height: log_h };
1089	    let input_area = Rect { x: inner.x, y: inner.y + log_h, width: inner.width, height: 1 };
1090	
1091	    let visible = log_h as usize;
1092	    // Show the most recent N lines (cap by visible height) so the buffer
1093	    // stays scrolled to the bottom on every render.
1094	    let start = n.chat.len().saturating_sub(visible);
1095	    let mut log_lines: Vec<Line> = Vec::with_capacity(visible);
1096	    if n.chat.is_empty() {
1097	        log_lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
1098	            "(no messages yet)",
1099	            TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
1100	        )));
1101	    } else {
1102	        for line in n.chat.iter().skip(start) {
1103	            log_lines.push(format_chat_line(line));
1104	        }
1105	    }
1106	    let log_para = Paragraph::new(log_lines).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
1107	    frame.render_widget(log_para, log_area);
1108	
1109	    let input_line = if input_active {
1110	        let buf = n.chat_input.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
1111	        Line::from(vec![
1112	            Span::styled("> ", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
1113	            Span::styled(format!("{}_", buf), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
1114	        ])
1115	    } else {
1116	        let hint = match n.role {
1117	            Some(NetRole::Spectator) => "(spectator — read-only)",
1118	            Some(NetRole::Player(_)) => "Press 't' to chat.",
1119	            None => "",
1120	        };
1121	        Line::from(Span::styled(hint, TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)))
1122	    };
1123	    frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(input_line).wrap(Wrap { trim: false }), input_area);
1124	}

1126	fn format_chat_line(line: &chess_net::ChatLine) -> Line<'static> {
```

```text
1217	/// Compact, human-readable rendering of `GameStatus` for the sidebar's

1220	fn format_status_short(status: GameStatus, style: Style) -> (String, Color) {

1239	fn draw_rules_overlay(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, _style: Style) {
1240	    // Center an overlay roughly 70 cols × 24 rows over the screen.
1241	    let pad_x = area.width.saturating_sub(72) / 2;
1242	    let pad_y = area.height.saturating_sub(26) / 2;
1243	    let overlay = Rect {
1244	        x: area.x + pad_x,
1245	        y: area.y + pad_y,
1246	        width: area.width.min(72),
1247	        height: area.height.min(26),
1248	    };
1249	    frame.render_widget(Clear, overlay);
1250	    let block =
1251	        Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Rules / 規則 — press r or Esc to close ");
1252	    let lines: Vec<Line> = RULES_LINES
1253	        .iter()
1254	        .map(|(s, accent)| {
1255	            let style = if *accent {
1256	                TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)
1257	            } else {
1258	                TuiStyle::default()
1259	            };
1260	            Line::from(Span::styled(*s, style))
1261	        })
1262	        .collect();
1263	    let para = Paragraph::new(lines).block(block).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
1264	    frame.render_widget(para, overlay);
1265	}
```

```text
1	//! Tiny text-input helpers for the host/lobby/create-room prompts.
2	//!
3	//! Deliberately not pulling in the `tui-input` crate — these inputs are
4	//! short ASCII strings (host:port, room id, password) and a 30-line helper
5	//! covers every keypress we care about.
6	
7	/// Append a printable char, capped at `max_len` bytes. Non-printables and
8	/// over-budget chars are silently dropped.
9	pub fn push_char(buf: &mut String, ch: char, max_len: usize) {
10	    if !ch.is_control() && buf.len() + ch.len_utf8() <= max_len {
11	        buf.push(ch);
12	    }
13	}
14	
15	/// Pop the last char (or no-op if empty).
16	pub fn backspace(buf: &mut String) {
17	    buf.pop();
18	}
19	
20	/// Mask a password for display: `••••••` (one `•` per char) up to 32, then
21	/// `…` for any extra. Empty becomes the placeholder `(none)`.
22	pub fn mask(pw: &str) -> String {
23	    if pw.is_empty() {
24	        return "(none)".into();
25	    }
26	    let n = pw.chars().count();
27	    if n <= 32 {
28	        "•".repeat(n)
29	    } else {
30	        format!("{}…", "•".repeat(31))
31	    }
32	}
33
```

```text
1	//! Per-player view projection.
2	//!
3	//! `PlayerView` is the only state the network layer should serialize and
4	//! ship to clients — it has hidden-piece identities scrubbed and (for
5	//! non-side-to-move observers) no legal-move list to leak strategy.
6	//!
7	//! See ADR-0004.
8	
9	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
10	
11	use crate::board::BoardShape;
12	use crate::moves::{Move, MoveList};
13	use crate::piece::{PieceOnSquare, Side};
14	use crate::state::{GameState, GameStatus};
15	
16	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
17	pub enum VisibleCell {
18	    Empty,
19	    /// Banqi face-down: identity hidden from this observer.
20	    Hidden,
21	    Revealed(PieceOnSquare),
22	}
23	
24	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
25	pub struct PlayerView {
26	    pub observer: Side,
27	    pub shape: BoardShape,
28	    pub width: u8,
29	    pub height: u8,
30	    pub cells: Vec<VisibleCell>,
31	    pub side_to_move: Side,
32	    pub status: GameStatus,
33	    /// Legal moves for the observer if it's their turn; empty otherwise.
34	    pub legal_moves: MoveList,
35	}
36	
37	impl PlayerView {
38	    /// Project `state` from `observer`'s vantage point. Hidden pieces stay
39	    /// hidden; for opponents, legal-move list is empty.
40	    pub fn project(state: &GameState, observer: Side) -> Self {
41	        let board = &state.board;
42	        let cells: Vec<VisibleCell> = (0..board.cell_count())
43	            .map(|i| {
44	                let sq = crate::coord::Square(i as u16);
45	                match board.get(sq) {
46	                    None => VisibleCell::Empty,
47	                    Some(pos) if pos.revealed => VisibleCell::Revealed(pos),
48	                    Some(_) => VisibleCell::Hidden,
49	                }
50	            })
51	            .collect();
52	
53	        let legal_moves = if observer == state.side_to_move {
54	            sanitize_for_observer(state.legal_moves(), observer)
55	        } else {
56	            MoveList::new()
57	        };
58	
59	        Self {
60	            observer,
61	            shape: board.shape(),
62	            width: board.width(),
63	            height: board.height(),
64	            cells,
65	            side_to_move: state.side_to_move,
66	            status: state.status,
67	            legal_moves,
68	        }
69	    }
70	}
71	
72	/// Strip identity from `Reveal` moves before they reach the network.
73	/// Even the side-to-move sees `revealed: None` because the engine resolves
74	/// the identity authoritatively when the move is applied.
75	fn sanitize_for_observer(moves: MoveList, _observer: Side) -> MoveList {
76	    moves
77	        .into_iter()
78	        .map(|m| match m {
79	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: _ } => Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None },
80	            other => other,
81	        })
82	        .collect()
83	}
84	
85	#[cfg(test)]
86	mod tests {
87	    use super::*;
88	    use crate::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
89	
90	    #[test]
```
</details></tool-use>

83	
84	TUI input map: `hjkl` / arrows move cursor, `Enter` / `Space` select-or-commit,
85	`Esc` cancel, `u` undo, `f` flip (banqi), `n` new game (back to picker),
86	`r` toggle rules overlay, `?` toggle keymap help, `q` / `Ctrl-C` quit. In
87	Net mode, `t` opens chat input (players only — Enter sends, Esc cancels);
88	in the lobby, `w` watches the highlighted room as a spectator.
89	Mouse left-click selects or commits. When the game ends, a banner appears in
90	the sidebar and move attempts are gated; `n` returns to the picker, `u` takes
91	back the losing move.
92	
93	`rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown` once per machine. If your rustup mirror lacks the target (e.g. tuna), prefix the command with `RUSTUP_DIST_SERVER=https://static.rust-lang.org` — see [`pitfalls/wasm-getrandom-unresolved-imp.md`](pitfalls/wasm-getrandom-unresolved-imp.md) for the related `js`-feature gotcha.
94	
95	## Architecture quick reference
96	
97	The engine lives entirely in `crates/chess-core`. `chess-tui` consumes it for local play and (via `--connect`) talks to `chess-net`'s axum-ws server, which holds the authoritative `GameState` and broadcasts per-side `PlayerView` after each move. `chess-engine`, `chess-ai`, `chess-web`, and `xtask` are still stubs. Five non-obvious decisions are locked in — full rationale in `docs/adr/`:
98	
99	1. **`Square(u16)` linear index** (ADR-0002), not `(file, rank)` tuples. `Board` knows its `BoardShape` and converts. Scales to 19×19 + irregular topology via per-shape mask.
100	2. **`Move` is a flat enum** (ADR-0004). `Move::Reveal { at, revealed: Option<Piece> }` is the network ABI boundary: clients send `revealed: None`, the authoritative engine fills in `Some(piece)` post-flip. All variants serde clean.
101	3. **`RuleSet` is plain data + `bitflags`, not a trait** (ADR-0003). Move-gen is free functions in `rules/{xiangqi,banqi,three_kingdom}.rs` dispatching on `Variant` and consulting `HouseRules` flags. Trait-object rule layering was rejected — kills inlining, fights serde.
102	4. **`GameState` is one concrete struct.** `TurnOrder` holds `SmallVec<[Side; 3]>` so 3-player isn't a special case. `Side(u8)` (not a fixed enum) carries this.
103	5. **`PlayerView::project(&GameState, observer)` is the only externally-visible state.** Hidden pieces become `VisibleCell::Hidden` with no identity. `tests/view_projection.rs` proptest enforces no-leak in serialized JSON. The future network layer must ship `PlayerView`, never `GameState`.
104	
105	Move generation pipeline (xiangqi): `pseudo_legal_moves` (geometry only) → clone-the-state-and-probe legality filter → emit. Cheap enough for 9×10; a future AI hot path should switch to make/unmake without cloning.
106	
107	## Gotchas worth knowing
108	
109	- **`make_move` does NOT auto-refresh `status`.** The xiangqi legality filter calls `make_move` on a clone to test self-check; auto-refresh would recurse via `legal_moves`. Callers (CLI / TUI / future server) invoke `state.refresh_status()` after each move when they want to know if the game ended. `refresh_status` covers no-progress draws + no-legal-moves; threefold repetition is a TODO.
110	
111	- **Three deferred house rules accept the flag but no-op**: `DARK_CHAIN`, `HORSE_DIAGONAL`, `CANNON_FAST_MOVE`. Only `CHAIN_CAPTURE` and `CHARIOT_RUSH` are wired end-to-end. Don't assume code that consumes `HouseRules` handles every flag — grep `rules/banqi.rs::generate` to confirm. The deferred ones are P1 in `TODO.md`.
112	
113	- **`Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi` exists but produces an empty 4×8 board.** The types are ready (3-seat `TurnOrder`, `Side(2)`, `BoardShape::ThreeKingdom`), but the actual mask + capture rules ship in PR 2. The setup builder is `setup.rs::build_three_kingdom_stub`. See `backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md` for what the implementation needs to settle.
114	
115	- **WASM build needs `getrandom = { features = ["js"] }`** for `wasm32-unknown-unknown` browser builds. `chess-core/Cargo.toml` adds this as a target-specific dep. Symptom and root cause documented in `pitfalls/wasm-getrandom-unresolved-imp.md`.
116	
117	- **Move list is `SmallVec<[Move; 32]>`.** Positions exceeding 32 legal moves spill to heap once — fine for correctness but watch when doing AI work.
118	
119	- **Banqi shuffle determinism.** `RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(house, seed)` uses `ChaCha8Rng` (deterministic). `RuleSet::banqi(house)` falls back to `rand::thread_rng()` for the seed — fine on native; in browser WASM works because of the `js`-feature dep above.
120	
121	- **Perft is the canary** for move-gen regressions. `tests/xiangqi_perft.rs` locks depth 1 = 44, depth 2 = 1920, depth 3 = 79666 (matches published values). If any of those change, audit the rule edit before assuming the test is wrong.
122	
123	- **Test fixtures use `.pos` DSL.** Hand-written positions live in `tests/fixtures/<variant>/*.pos` and load via `GameState::from_pos_text(&str)`. `tests/end_conditions.rs` shows the pattern. Format spec: [`docs/snapshot-format.md`](docs/snapshot-format.md). Don't put new test positions inline as Rust code if a fixture file would do — fixtures are editable, diff-friendly, and double as endgame-puzzle source files.
124	
125	- **Replay = `(initial, moves[])` not `Vec<MoveRecord>`.** `Replay::from_game(state, meta)` walks `state.history` back to the start via `unmake_move` and records the moves. `Replay::play_to(step)` is the single primitive behind animation playback, multi-ply takeback, fork-from-midpoint, and endgame puzzle "start at this position" — don't reinvent any of those.
126	
127	- **chess-tui orientation lives in `clients/chess-tui/src/orient.rs`, not chess-core.** The engine stays presentation-free; the renderer transposes banqi (4×8 model → 8×4 display) and flips xiangqi (rank 0 at the bottom for Red observer, top for Black) entirely client-side. When `chess-net` lands, the same `project_cell` / `square_at_display` pair handles per-player perspective without any engine change.
128	
129	- **chess-tui board uses an intersection layout, not boxed cells.** Pieces sit on grid crossings (rendered as `┼` for empty intersections, `╳` at palace centers, or the piece glyph). Rank rows are interleaved with between-rows containing `│` verticals plus `╲ ╱` palace diagonals. The river replaces the between-row at index 4 with a stylised text band — no vertical lines pass through it. Each terminal "cell" spans 4 cols × 2 rows; mouse hit-test in `app.rs::hit_test` divides by these constants. ASCII fallback (`--style ascii`) maps the same layout onto `+ - | \ / X` chars.
130	
131	- **Casual xiangqi (`RuleSet::xiangqi_casual()` / `xiangqi_allow_self_check: true`)** disables the standard self-check legality filter. Moves that leave your general capturable are accepted; the game ends with `WinReason::GeneralCaptured` when the general is physically taken. `refresh_status` detects the missing general unconditionally — keep the existing checkmate-by-zero-legal-moves path intact (it's still reachable in standard mode). When adding a new RuleSet field, mark it `#[serde(default)]` so older snapshots still deserialize. The TUI defaults to casual; the engine `RuleSet::xiangqi()` factory is still strict (so existing engine tests / snapshots stay correct) — only the chess-tui picker / `Cmd::Xiangqi` selection picks `xiangqi_casual()` by default.
132	
133	- **`chess-net` is multi-room (ADR-0005) with v3 spectator + chat (ADR-0006).** `crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs` defines `ServerMsg`/`ClientMsg` (JSON over text frames, `#[serde(tag = "type")]`). Routes: `GET /` and `GET /ws` upgrade into the default room `main` (backwards compat with v1 clients); `GET /ws/<room-id>` upgrades into a named room (auto-created on first arrival, GC'd when the last seat **and** spectator leaves — except `main`, which is permanent); `GET /lobby` is a non-seated subscription that receives `Rooms` pushes on every state change; `GET /rooms` returns the same snapshot as JSON for `curl`/debugging. Optional `?password=<secret>` locks the room (applies to seats and spectators); wrong password → `ServerMsg::Error{"bad password"}` before any welcome. **Password is plain-text friend-lock, not security** — there's no WSS or auth in the loop. Server holds `Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<Mutex<RoomState>>>>>` + parallel `summaries` cache so `notify_lobby` builds `Rooms` snapshots without holding inner locks. Within a room, first seat = Red, second = Black; third+ joiner without `?role=spectator` still gets `room full` (v2 back-compat). `chess-tui` joins via `--connect` (direct URL) or `--lobby <host>` (room browser); the lobby spawns a second sync `tungstenite` worker — no tokio in the TUI binary. `Move::Reveal` stays `revealed: None` on the wire end-to-end (the server fills `Some(...)` only inside its local state). Reconnect / time controls / takeback / mixed-variant rooms / TLS are deferred (see `TODO.md`).
134	
135	- **chess-net v3 spectator opt-in is `?role=spectator` (explicit only).** Joining a room with the param routes to the spectator branch in `server.rs::handle_room_socket`: server sends `ServerMsg::Spectating { protocol, rules, view }` (`PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED)`), then `ChatHistory { lines }` from the room's ring buffer. Spectators receive `Update` from RED's perspective on every move — banqi hidden tiles stay opaque per ADR-0004. Move / Resign / Rematch / Chat from a spectator returns the appropriate `Error{"spectators cannot ..."}`. **Cap defaults to 16 per room**; override with `--max-spectators N` / `CHESS_NET_MAX_SPECTATORS` env. The (cap+1)th spectator gets `Error{"room watch capacity reached"}`. Spectators of a `?password=`-locked room must present the same password as players (validated before role branching). `RoomSummary.spectators: u16` is `#[serde(default)]` for v2 lobby back-compat. Auto-fallback (silently upgrading a third joiner to spectator) was rejected in ADR-0006 because v2 chess-tui clients would crash on the `Spectating` JSON tag — explicit opt-in keeps the v2 path byte-identical.
136	
137	- **chess-net chat is players-only with a 50-line per-room ring buffer.** `ClientMsg::Chat { text }` from a seat: trimmed, control chars (`\n` / `\t`) collapsed to space, capped at 256 chars, server-stamps `ts_ms`, pushes to `RoomState.chat: VecDeque<ChatLine>` (oldest dropped at cap), then `broadcast_to_all` fans `ServerMsg::Chat { line }` to seats + spectators with the same payload (chat is a single broadcast, unlike `Update` which projects per-seat). Same `text` from a spectator → `Error{"spectators cannot chat"}`. Late joiners get the buffer in their welcome `ChatHistory { lines }`. **No rate limit, no moderation, no encryption** — this is a friend-only channel; production-grade primitives are sketched at `backlog/chess-net-chat-moderation.md`. System messages (player joined / left) are `ChatLine.from`'s next axis — see `backlog/chess-net-system-messages.md` (will bump to v4 when shipped).
138	
139	- **chess-tui chat-input mode hijacks the keymap.** Pressing `t` in Net mode sets `NetView.chat_input = Some(String::new())`; `app.input_mode()` then returns `InputMode::Text` so the dispatcher treats printable keys as edits. `Enter` sends `ClientMsg::Chat`; `Esc` (Action::Back) cancels. The chat region in `draw_sidebar_net` reserves the bottom rows of the sidebar (`MIN_CHAT_ROWS = 6`); the log auto-scrolls by always rendering the last `log_h` lines from the `VecDeque`. Spectators see "(spectator — read-only)" instead of the input row and pressing `t` shows a hint. Lobby key `w` triggers `Action::LobbyWatch` → connects with `?role=spectator` (locked rooms still prompt for the password via the existing `PendingJoin` flow, now extended with an `as_spectator: bool` field).
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 07:13:29Z)**_

Excellent. I have all the facts I need. Now I'll produce the implementation plan.

---

# TUI Coordinate-Based Move Input — Implementation Plan

## Goal recap

Add two trigger keys in `InputMode::Game` for typing ICCS moves directly:

- **`:`** — *Instant prompt* mode. Text-only buffer; no live preview; Enter commits; Esc cancels.
- **`m`** — *Live preview* mode. Each keystroke re-parses; a 2-char prefix sets `selected = Some(square)` so the existing selected-square highlight kicks in; a full 4-char move also moves the cursor onto the destination square so the cursor highlight previews the target. Esc restores the cursor + selection that existed before `m` was pressed.

Both work in **Local Game** (`Screen::Game`) and **Net** (`Screen::Net`); spectators are gated with the existing `"Spectators cannot move."` message and the prompt never opens.

---

## 1. State additions

A single shared struct, parameterized by mode, lives directly on each view (mirrors `chat_input: Option<String>`).

**New helper enum + struct in `clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs`:**

```rust
#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
pub enum CoordKind { Instant, Live }

pub struct CoordInputState {
    pub kind: CoordKind,
    pub buf: String,
    /// Live mode only: (cursor, selected) at entry, restored on Esc.
    /// Always `None` when `kind == Instant`.
    pub snapshot: Option<((u8, u8), Option<Square>)>,
}
```

Place it next to `PendingJoin` and `CreateRoomField`; make the struct fields `pub` so dispatchers and the renderer can read them.

**`GameView`** (line 80-85) gains:
```rust
pub coord_input: Option<CoordInputState>,
```

**`NetView`** (line 115-133) gains the same field after `chat_input`:
```rust
pub coord_input: Option<CoordInputState>,
```

Initialize to `None` in `AppState::new_game` and `AppState::new_net`. Reset to `None` whenever either view is rebuilt.

**Why on the view, not separate**: the mutual-exclusion gate against `chat_input` (Net only) is cleanest when both fields sit on the same struct; the `input_mode()` and `dispatch_back()` arms can do `n.chat_input.is_some() || n.coord_input.is_some()` in one match.

**Buffer cap**: `const COORD_INPUT_MAX: usize = 16;` near `CHAT_INPUT_MAX` (longest realistic ICCS string is `flip a0` = 7 chars; chain-capture `a3xb3xc3xd3` = 11; 16 leaves headroom).

---

## 2. Action additions

**Single new variant** in `clients/chess-tui/src/input.rs`:

```rust
/// Open the coordinate-input prompt (Game / Net, players only).
CoordStart(CoordKind),
```

Plus `pub use` of `CoordKind` from `app.rs` — actually cleaner to **define `CoordKind` inside `input.rs`** and re-export it from `app.rs` (avoids a circular dep since `app.rs` already imports from `input.rs`). Move:

```rust
// in input.rs
#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
pub enum CoordKind { Instant, Live }
```

Then `app.rs` imports it via `use crate::input::{Action, CoordKind, InputMode};` and defines `CoordInputState` locally.

**Keymap delta** in `from_key` `InputMode::Game` arm (around line 120, alongside `'t' => ChatStart`):

```rust
KeyCode::Char(':') => Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Instant),
KeyCode::Char('m') => Action::CoordStart(CoordKind::Live),
```

No InputMode::Text changes — the Text branch already handles `TextInput / TextBackspace / Submit / Back`.

---

## 3. `input_mode()` extension

In `app.rs:355-371`, extend the `Net(n)` branch and add a `Game(g)` branch (currently only `chat_input` is checked):

```rust
Screen::Net(n) if n.chat_input.is_some() || n.coord_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
Screen::Game(g) if g.coord_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
Screen::Game(_) | Screen::Net(_) => InputMode::Game,
```

---

## 4. Action routing in `dispatch()`

Add a new arm in `AppState::dispatch` (line 373-454), parallel to `ChatStart`:

```rust
Action::CoordStart(kind) => self.dispatch_coord_start(kind),
```

---

## 5. New `dispatch_coord_start`

Mirrors `dispatch_chat_start` (line 486-502) but spans both screens.

```rust
fn dispatch_coord_start(&mut self, kind: CoordKind) {
    match &mut self.screen {
        Screen::Game(g) => {
            // Local: open unconditionally; engine gates illegal moves on submit.
            // (mirror of handle_select_or_commit's GameStatus::Ongoing check is
            // applied at submit time, not entry time — keeps the prompt useful
            // for, e.g., post-game inspection of typing.)
            g.coord_input = Some(CoordInputState {
                kind,
                buf: String::new(),
                snapshot: match kind {
                    CoordKind::Live => Some((g.cursor, g.selected)),
                    CoordKind::Instant => None,
                },
            });
            // Live mode clears the highlight to start clean; Instant leaves it.
            if matches!(kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                g.selected = None;
            }
            g.last_msg = Some(coord_help_msg(kind));
        }
        Screen::Net(n) => {
            // Mutual exclusion with chat-input.
            if n.chat_input.is_some() {
                n.last_msg = Some("Finish or cancel chat (Esc) before move-input.".into());
                return;
            }
            // Spectator gate (mirrors line 495-497).
            match n.role {
                Some(NetRole::Spectator) => {
                    n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
                    return;
                }
                None => {
                    n.last_msg = Some("Not connected yet.".into());
                    return;
                }
                Some(NetRole::Player(_)) => {}
            }
            n.coord_input = Some(CoordInputState {
                kind,
                buf: String::new(),
                snapshot: match kind {
                    CoordKind::Live => Some((n.cursor, n.selected)),
                    CoordKind::Instant => None,
                },
            });
            if matches!(kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                n.selected = None;
            }
            n.last_msg = Some(coord_help_msg(kind));
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}

fn coord_help_msg(kind: CoordKind) -> String {
    match kind {
        CoordKind::Instant => "Coord (instant): type ICCS, Enter commits, Esc cancels.".into(),
        CoordKind::Live    => "Coord (live): type ICCS, Enter commits, Esc cancels.".into(),
    }
}
```

Mutual-exclusion in the other direction: extend `dispatch_chat_start` with `if n.coord_input.is_some() { n.last_msg = Some("Finish or cancel coord-input (Esc) before chat.".into()); return; }` after the `Screen::Net(n)` early-let.

---

## 6. `dispatch_text` extension

Extend the `Screen::Net(n)` branch (line 731-750) and add a `Screen::Game(g)` branch.

For **Net**, replace the `let Some(buf) = n.chat_input.as_mut() else { return; };` with a dispatch on which input mode is active:

```rust
Screen::Net(n) => {
    if n.coord_input.is_some() {
        Self::dispatch_coord_text_net(n, action);
    } else if n.chat_input.is_some() {
        // existing chat branch verbatim
    }
}
```

For **Game**, add:

```rust
Screen::Game(g) => {
    if g.coord_input.is_some() {
        let observer = self.observer;
        Self::dispatch_coord_text_game(g, action, observer);
    }
}
```

### 6a. Local-game variant `dispatch_coord_text_game`

```rust
fn dispatch_coord_text_game(g: &mut GameView, action: Action, observer: Side) {
    let Some(ci) = g.coord_input.as_mut() else { return; };
    match action {
        Action::TextInput(c) => {
            text_input::push_char(&mut ci.buf, c, COORD_INPUT_MAX);
            if matches!(ci.kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                Self::live_preview_game(g, observer);
            }
        }
        Action::TextBackspace => {
            text_input::backspace(&mut ci.buf);
            if matches!(ci.kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                Self::live_preview_game(g, observer);
            }
        }
        Action::Submit => {
            // Take ownership of buf, leave struct in place for error case.
            let buf = std::mem::take(&mut ci.buf);
            match chess_core::notation::iccs::decode_move(&g.state, buf.trim()) {
                Ok(mv) => {
                    g.coord_input = None;
                    Self::apply_move(g, mv);
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    // Error: keep the prompt open so the user can fix; restore buf.
                    if let Some(ci) = g.coord_input.as_mut() {
                        ci.buf = buf;
                    }
                    g.last_msg = Some(format!("Bad move: {e}"));
                }
            }
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

`live_preview_game` re-runs the buffer through a small parse helper (see §10) and updates `g.selected` and (on a 4-char success) `g.cursor` via `orient::project_cell(sq, observer, shape)`.

### 6b. Net variant `dispatch_coord_text_net`

```rust
fn dispatch_coord_text_net(n: &mut NetView, action: Action) {
    let Some(ci) = n.coord_input.as_mut() else { return; };
    match action {
        Action::TextInput(c) => {
            text_input::push_char(&mut ci.buf, c, COORD_INPUT_MAX);
            if matches!(ci.kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                live_preview_net(n);
            }
        }
        Action::TextBackspace => {
            text_input::backspace(&mut ci.buf);
            if matches!(ci.kind, CoordKind::Live) {
                live_preview_net(n);
            }
        }
        Action::Submit => {
            // Pre-flight gates: turn / status / spectator (last redundant — gate
            // on entry — but harmless and protects against role changes mid-edit).
            let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else {
                n.last_msg = Some("Not connected yet.".into());
                return;
            };
            let role = n.role.unwrap_or(NetRole::Player(Side::RED));
            if role.is_spectator() {
                n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot move.".into());
                n.coord_input = None;
                return;
            }
            if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
                n.last_msg = Some("Game over.".into());
                n.coord_input = None;
                return;
            }
            if view.side_to_move != role.observer() {
                n.last_msg = Some("Not your turn.".into());
                return; // keep buf so user can wait + resubmit
            }
            let buf = std::mem::take(&mut ci.buf);
            match chess_core::notation::iccs::decode_move_from_view(view, buf.trim()) {
                Ok(mv) => {
                    n.coord_input = None;
                    n.selected = None;
                    let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
                    n.last_msg = Some("Sent.".into());
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    if let Some(ci) = n.coord_input.as_mut() {
                        ci.buf = buf;
                    }
                    n.last_msg = Some(format!("Bad move: {e}"));
                }
            }
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

### 6c. Live preview helpers

```rust
fn live_preview_game(g: &mut GameView, observer: Side) {
    let Some(ci) = g.coord_input.as_ref() else { return; };
    let shape = g.state.board.shape();
    let board = &g.state.board;
    apply_live_preview(
        &ci.buf,
        |s| parse_square_from_board(board, s),
        shape,
        observer,
        &mut g.cursor,
        &mut g.selected,
    );
}

fn live_preview_net(n: &mut NetView) {
    let Some(ci) = n.coord_input.as_ref() else { return; };
    let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else { return; };
    let shape = view.shape;
    let observer = n.role.map(|r| r.observer()).unwrap_or(Side::RED);
    apply_live_preview(
        &ci.buf,
        |s| parse_square_from_view(view, s),
        shape,
        observer,
        &mut n.cursor,
        &mut n.selected,
    );
}

/// Re-parse `buf` and update (selected, cursor) for live preview.
/// - 0–1 chars or first char invalid    → selected = None, cursor unchanged
/// - 2–3 chars with valid square prefix → selected = Some(origin), cursor unchanged
/// - 4 chars with valid from+to         → selected = Some(origin), cursor = display_at(to)
/// - "flip <sq>" or chain (5+)          → selected stays at last valid origin
fn apply_live_preview<F>(
    buf: &str,
    mut parse_sq: F,
    shape: BoardShape,
    observer: Side,
    cursor: &mut (u8, u8),
    selected: &mut Option<Square>,
) where F: FnMut(&str) -> Option<Square> {
    let trimmed = buf.trim_start_matches("flip ");
    let bare = trimmed.split('x').next().unwrap_or("");
    let len = trimmed.chars().count();
    // Drop highlight on a clean / too-short buffer.
    if buf.is_empty() || (len < 2 && !buf.starts_with("flip")) {
        *selected = None;
        return;
    }
    if let Some(origin) = parse_sq(&trimmed[..trimmed.len().min(2)]) {
        *selected = Some(origin);
        // Full 4-char form (and not "flip "): also jump the cursor.
        if buf.len() == 4 && !buf.contains('x') {
            if let Some(dest) = parse_sq(&trimmed[2..4]) {
                let (r, c) = orient::project_cell(dest, observer, shape);
                *cursor = (r, c);
            }
        }
        // After x-separator (e.g. "h2xh9"): also jump the cursor on the first hop.
        if buf.contains('x') {
            let parts: Vec<&str> = buf.split('x').collect();
            if parts.len() == 2 && parts[1].len() == 2 {
                if let Some(dest) = parse_sq(parts[1]) {
                    let (r, c) = orient::project_cell(dest, observer, shape);
                    *cursor = (r, c);
                }
            }
        }
        let _ = bare;
    } else {
        *selected = None;
    }
}
```

(The function deliberately stays syntactically tolerant: parse failures simply don't advance the highlight; the user can backspace and retry without an error popup.)

---

## 7. `dispatch_back` extension

Add coord-cancel arms before the chat arm at line 478:

```rust
Screen::Game(g) if g.coord_input.is_some() => {
    let ci = g.coord_input.take().unwrap();
    if let Some((cur, sel)) = ci.snapshot {
        g.cursor = cur;
        g.selected = sel;
    }
    g.last_msg = None;
}
Screen::Net(n) if n.coord_input.is_some() => {
    let ci = n.coord_input.take().unwrap();
    if let Some((cur, sel)) = ci.snapshot {
        n.cursor = cur;
        n.selected = sel;
    }
    n.last_msg = None;
}
// existing chat arm:
Screen::Net(n) if n.chat_input.is_some() => { … }
```

Order matters: coord-cancel arms come **before** the chat-cancel arm so the active coord prompt wins over a (impossible by mutual exclusion, but defensively) lingering chat field.

For Instant mode `snapshot` is `None` so no restore; cursor + selected stay as they were since live-preview never touched them.

---

## 8. Where the parsing logic lives

**Recommend (a)**: add `chess_core::notation::iccs::decode_move_from_view` and refactor `decode_move` to share. Reasoning:

- The TUI already pulls `chess-core::notation::iccs` for `encode_move` and `encode_square`; adding a sibling decode is a tiny surface change.
- Keeps the Step/Capture/CannonJump precedence logic in one place; the `chain_capture` resolver is the most subtle bit and should not be duplicated.
- `chess-web` will eventually want the same — putting it in chess-core makes it reusable.

### chess-core change

Refactor `iccs.rs` so the legal-move-resolution part takes a slice and a board, not a `&GameState`:

```rust
// Existing public entry stays, but delegates.
pub fn decode_move(state: &GameState, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
    decode_move_inner(&state.board, &state.legal_moves(), input)
}

pub fn decode_move_from_view(view: &PlayerView, input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
    // Build a temporary Board-like from view.shape so parse_square works.
    // PlayerView already has width/height/shape. parse_square only consults
    // board.width()/height() and board.sq(File, Rank). We can either:
    //   (i) add `parse_square_dims(width, height, shape, s)` and use it, or
    //   (ii) reconstruct a Board::empty(view.shape) for parsing only.
    // (i) is cheaper. Suggested signature:
    decode_move_dims(view.shape, view.width, view.height, &view.legal_moves, input)
}

fn decode_move_inner(board: &Board, legal: &[Move], input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> { ... }
fn decode_move_dims(shape: BoardShape, w: u8, h: u8, legal: &[Move], input: &str) -> Result<Move, CoreError> { ... }
```

Alternative: introduce a private `parse_square_dims(width: u8, height: u8, shape: BoardShape, s: &str) -> Result<Square, CoreError>` (the existing `parse_square` only differs in that it reads `board.width()/height()` and constructs `Square` via `board.sq(File, Rank)` — the latter is just `Square((rank * width + file) as u16)` for non-Custom shapes; check `board::sq` to confirm it has shape-dependent masking and, if so, also expose `BoardShape::sq(file, rank) -> Square` or pass `shape` and reuse).

### Add `PlayerView` to the import surface in iccs.rs

```rust
use crate::view::PlayerView;
```

### Tests in chess-core

Mirror the existing `parse_h2e2_in_xiangqi` test using `PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED)` instead of `&state` and assert the same Move comes back.

## 11. CLAUDE.md updates

### 11a. Input-map paragraph (line 84-91)

Append a sentence:
> `:` opens an instant coord-input prompt (type ICCS like `h2e2` / `a3xb3xc3` / `flip a0`, Enter commits, Esc cancels); `m` opens the same with live preview — every keystroke updates the selected-square highlight, and a complete 4-char move also previews the destination via the cursor.

### 11b. Gotchas section (after line 139, the chat-input gotcha)

Add:
> - **chess-tui coord-input mode also hijacks the keymap (Game + Net).** `:` and `m` set `coord_input = Some(CoordInputState { … })`; `app.input_mode()` returns `InputMode::Text`. `m` snapshots `(cursor, selected)` so Esc can restore the highlight; `:` does not. **Coord-input and chat-input are mutually exclusive in Net** — opening one with the other active is rejected with a `last_msg`. Decoding goes through `chess_core::notation::iccs::decode_move` (Local) or `decode_move_from_view` (Net) — the latter operates on a `&PlayerView` and shares its parser internals with the former. Submit on illegal/bad notation keeps the prompt open with an error in `last_msg`; Submit on a valid move clears the prompt and either calls `apply_move` (Local) or sends `ClientMsg::Move` (Net). Live-preview's cursor jump uses `orient::project_cell`, the long-marked `#[allow(dead_code)]` helper now finally pulls its weight.
